Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Presiding in Parliament
articles.simplified: (1) At any sitting of a House of Parliament— (a) the Speaker presides; (b) in the absence of the Speaker, the Deputy Speaker presides; and (c) in the absence of the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker, another member of the House elected by the House presides. (2) At a joint sitting of the Houses of Parliament, the Speaker of the National Assembly must preside, assisted by the Speaker of the Senate.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu presiding in Bunge. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) At any sitting of a House of Parliament— (a) the Speaker presides; (b) in the absence of the Speaker, the Deputy Speaker presides; and (c) in the absence of the Speaker and the Deputy Speaker, another member of the House elected by the House presides. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Presiding in Parliament, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) At a joint sitting of the Houses of Parliament, the Speaker of the National Assembly must preside, assisted by the Speaker of the Senate. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Presiding in Parliament, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu presiding in Bunge.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu presiding in Bunge.

articles.title: Party leaders
articles.simplified: (1) There must be a leader of the majority party and a leader of the minority party. (2) The leader of the majority party must be the person who is the leader in the National Assembly of the largest party or coalition of parties. (3) The leader of the minority party must be the person who is the leader in the National Assembly of the second largest party or coalition of parties.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu party viongozi. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There must be a leader of the majority party and a leader of the minority party. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Party leaders, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The leader of the majority party must be the person who is the leader in the National Assembly of the largest party or coalition of parties. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Party leaders, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu party viongozi.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu party viongozi.

articles.title: Exercise of legislative powers
articles.simplified: (1) Parliament must exercise its legislative power through Bills passed by Parliament and assented to by the President. (2) Any Bill can originate in the National Assembly. (3) A Bill not concerning county government is considered only in the National Assembly, and passed in accordance with Article 122 and the Standing Orders of the Assembly.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu exercise of legislative mamlakas. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Parliament must exercise its legislative power through Bills passed by Parliament and assented to by the President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Exercise of legislative powers, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Any Bill can originate in the National Assembly. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Exercise of legislative powers, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu exercise of legislative mamlakas.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu exercise of legislative mamlakas.

articles.title: Bills concerning county government
articles.simplified: (1) In this Constitution, "a Bill concerning county government" means— (a) a Bill containing provisions affecting the functions and powers of the county governments set out in the Fourth Schedule; (b) a Bill relating to the election of members of a county assembly or a county executive; and (c) a Bill referred to in Chapter Twelve affecting the finances of county governments. (2) A Bill concerning county governments is— (a) a special Bill, which must be considered under Article 111, if it— (i) relates to the election of members of a county assembly or a county executive; or (ii) is the annual County Allocation of Revenue Bill referred to in Article 218; or (b) an ordinary Bill, which must be considered under Article 112, in any other case. (3) Before either House considers a Bill, the Speakers of the National Assembly and Senate must jointly resolve any question as to whether it is a Bill concerning counties and, if it is, whether it is a special or an ordinary Bill.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu bills concerning serikali ya kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) In this Constitution, "a Bill concerning county government" means— (a) a Bill containing provisions affecting the functions and powers of the county governments set out in the Fourth Schedule; (b) a Bill relating to the election of members of a county assembly or a county executive; and (c) a Bill referred to in Chapter Twelve affecting the finances of county governments. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Bills concerning county government, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) A Bill concerning county governments is— (a) a special Bill, which must be considered under Article 111, if it— (i) relates to the election of members of a county assembly or a county executive; or (ii) is the annual County Allocation of Revenue Bill referred to in Article 218; or (b) an ordinary Bill, which must be considered under Article 112, in any other case. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Bills concerning county government, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu bills concerning serikali ya kaunti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu bills concerning serikali ya kaunti.

articles.title: Special Bills concerning county governments
articles.swTitle: Special Bills concerning serikali za kaunti
articles.simplified: (1) A special Bill concerning a county government must proceed in the same manner as an ordinary Bill concerning county government, subject to clauses (2) and (3). (2) The National Assembly can amend or veto a special Bill that has been passed by the Senate only by a resolution supported by at least two-thirds of the members of the Assembly. (3) If a resolution in the National Assembly to amend or veto a special Bill fails to pass, the Speaker of the Assembly must, within seven days, refer the Bill, in the form adopted by the Senate, to the President for assent.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu special Bills concerning serikali za kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A special Bill concerning a county government must proceed in the same manner as an ordinary Bill concerning county government, subject to clauses (2) and (3). That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Special Bills concerning county governments, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The National Assembly can amend or veto a special Bill that has been passed by the Senate only by a resolution supported by at least two-thirds of the members of the Assembly. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Special Bills concerning county governments, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu special Bills concerning serikali za kaunti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu special Bills concerning serikali za kaunti.

articles.title: Ordinary Bills concerning county governments
articles.swTitle: Ordinary Bills concerning serikali za kaunti
articles.simplified: (1) If one House passes an ordinary Bill concerning counties, and the second House— (a) rejects the Bill, it must be referred to a mediation committee appointed under Article 113; or (b) passes the Bill in an amended form, it must be referred back to the originating House for reconsideration. (2) If, after the originating House has reconsidered a Bill referred back to it under clause (1)(b), that House— (a) passes the Bill as amended, the Speaker of that House must refer the Bill to the President within seven days for assent; or (b) rejects the Bill as amended, the Bill must be referred to a mediation committee under Article 113.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ordinary Bills concerning serikali za kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) If one House passes an ordinary Bill concerning counties, and the second House— (a) rejects the Bill, it must be referred to a mediation committee appointed under Article 113; or (b) passes the Bill in an amended form, it must be referred back to the originating House for reconsideration. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Ordinary Bills concerning county governments, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If, after the originating House has reconsidered a Bill referred back to it under clause (1)(b), that House— (a) passes the Bill as amended, the Speaker of that House must refer the Bill to the President within seven days for assent; or (b) rejects the Bill as amended, the Bill must be referred to a mediation committee under Article 113. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Ordinary Bills concerning county governments, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ordinary Bills concerning serikali za kaunti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ordinary Bills concerning serikali za kaunti.

articles.title: Mediation committees
articles.simplified: (1) If a Bill is referred to a mediation committee under Article 112, the Speakers of both Houses must appoint a mediation committee consisting of equal numbers of members of each House to attempt to develop a version of the Bill that both Houses will pass. (2) If the mediation committee agrees on a version of the Bill, each House must vote to approve or reject that version of the Bill. (3) If both Houses approve the version of the Bill proposed by the mediation committee, the Speaker of the National Assembly must refer the Bill to the President within seven days for assent.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu mediation committees. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) If a Bill is referred to a mediation committee under Article 112, the Speakers of both Houses must appoint a mediation committee consisting of equal numbers of members of each House to attempt to develop a version of the Bill that both Houses will pass. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Mediation committees, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If the mediation committee agrees on a version of the Bill, each House must vote to approve or reject that version of the Bill. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Mediation committees, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu mediation committees.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu mediation committees.

articles.title: Money Bills
articles.simplified: (1) A money Bill can not deal with any matter other than those listed in the definition of "a money Bill" in clause (3). (2) If, in the opinion of the Speaker of the National Assembly, a motion makes provision for a matter listed in the definition of "a money Bill", the Assembly can proceed only in accordance with the recommendation of the relevant Committee of the Assembly after taking into account the views of the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance. (3) In this Constitution, "a money Bill" means a Bill, other than a Bill specified in Article 218, that contains provisions dealing with— (a) taxes; (b) the imposition of charges on a public fund or the variation or repeal of any of those charges; (c) the appropriation, receipt, custody, investment or issue of public money; (d) the raising or guaranteeing of any loan or its repayment; or (e) matters incidental to any of those matters.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu money Bills. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A money Bill can not deal with any matter other than those listed in the definition of "a money Bill" in clause (3). That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Money Bills, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If, in the opinion of the Speaker of the National Assembly, a motion makes provision for a matter listed in the definition of "a money Bill", the Assembly can proceed only in accordance with the recommendation of the relevant Committee of the Assembly after taking into account the views of the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Money Bills, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu money Bills.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu money Bills.

articles.title: Presidential assent and referral
articles.simplified: (1) Within fourteen days after receipt of a Bill, the President must— (a) assent to the Bill; or (b) refer the Bill back to Parliament for reconsideration by Parliament, noting any reservations that the President has concerning the Bill. (2) If the President refers a Bill back for reconsideration, Parliament can, following the appropriate procedures under this Part— (a) amend the Bill in light of the President’s reservations; or (b) pass the Bill a second time without amendment. (3) If Parliament amends the Bill fully accommodating the President’s reservations, the appropriate Speaker must re-submit it to the President for assent.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Within fourteen days after receipt of a Bill, the President must— (a) assent to the Bill; or (b) refer the Bill back to Parliament for reconsideration by Parliament, noting any reservations that the President has concerning the Bill. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Presidential assent and referral, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If the President refers a Bill back for reconsideration, Parliament can, following the appropriate procedures under this Part— (a) amend the Bill in light of the President’s reservations; or (b) pass the Bill a second time without amendment. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Presidential assent and referral, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Coming into force of laws
articles.simplified: (1) A Bill passed by Parliament and assented to by the President must be published in the Gazette as an Act of Parliament within seven days after-assent. (2) Subject to clause (3), an Act of Parliament comes into force on the fourteenth day after its publication in the Gazette, unless the Act stipulates a different date on or time at which it will come into force. (3) An Act of Parliament that confers a direct pecuniary interest on members of Parliament must not come into force until after the next general election of members of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu coming into force of sheria. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A Bill passed by Parliament and assented to by the President must be published in the Gazette as an Act of Parliament within seven days after-assent. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Coming into force of laws, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Subject to clause (3), an Act of Parliament comes into force on the fourteenth day after its publication in the Gazette, unless the Act stipulates a different date on or time at which it will come into force. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Coming into force of laws, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu coming into force of sheria.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu coming into force of sheria.

articles.title: Powers, privileges and immunities
articles.simplified: (1) There must be freedom of speech and debate in Parliament. (2) Parliament can, for the purpose of the orderly and effective discharge of the business of Parliament, provide for the powers, privileges and immunities of Parliament, its committees, the leader of the majority party, the leader of the minority party, the chairpersons of committees and members.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There must be freedom of speech and debate in Parliament. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Powers, privileges and immunities, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament can, for the purpose of the orderly and effective discharge of the business of Parliament, provide for the powers, privileges and immunities of Parliament, its committees, the leader of the majority party, the leader of the minority party, the chairpersons of committees and members. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Powers, privileges and immunities, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Public
articles.simplified: Public participation.
articles.swSimplified: Public participation.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Parliament must— (a) conduct its business in an open manner, and its sittings and those of its committees must be in public; and (b) facilitate public participation and involvement in the legislative and other business of Parliament and its committees. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Public access and participation, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament can not exclude the public, or any media, from any sitting unless in exceptional circumstances the relevant Speaker has determined that there are justifiable reasons for the exclusion. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Public access and participation, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Petitions
articles.simplified: Right to petition Parliament to consider any matter within its authority.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) You has a right to petition Parliament to consider any matter within its authority, including to enact, amend or repeal any legislation. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Right to petition Parliament, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament must make provision for the procedure for the exercise of this right. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Right to petition Parliament, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Official languages of Parliament
articles.simplified: (1) The official languages of Parliament must be Kiswahili, English and Kenyan Sign language, and the business of Parliament can be conducted in English, Kiswahili and Kenyan Sign language. (2) In case of a conflict between different language versions of an Act of Parliament, the version signed by the President must prevail.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu official languages of Bunge. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Official languages of Parliament, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) In case of a conflict between different language versions of an Act of Parliament, the version signed by the President must prevail. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Official languages of Parliament, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu official languages of Bunge.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu official languages of Bunge.

articles.title: Quorum
articles.simplified: The quorum of Parliament must be– (a) fifty members, in the case of the National Assembly; or (b) fifteen members, in the case of the Senate. It also guides what officials must or must not do for you.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu quorum. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Quorum, that is how it should be handled.
Achieng is in a real situation: Quorum. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Quorum, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu quorum.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu quorum.

articles.title: Voting in Parliament
articles.simplified: (1) Except as otherwise provided in this Constitution, any question proposed for decision in either House of Parliament must be determined by a majority of the members in that House, present and voting. (2) On a question proposed for decision in either House— (a) the Speaker has no vote; and (b) in the case of a tie, the question is lost. (3) A member must not vote on any question in which the member has a pecuniary interest.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu kupiga kura in Bunge. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Except as otherwise provided in this Constitution, any question proposed for decision in either House of Parliament must be determined by a majority of the members in that House, present and voting. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Voting in Parliament, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) On a question proposed for decision in either House— (a) the Speaker has no vote; and (b) in the case of a tie, the question is lost. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Voting in Parliament, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu kupiga kura in Bunge.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu kupiga kura in Bunge.

articles.title: Decisions of Senate
articles.simplified: (1) On election, all the members of the Senate who were registered as voters in a particular county must collectively constitute a single delegation for purposes of clause (4) and the member elected under Article 98(1)(a) must be the head of the delegation. (2) When the Senate is to vote on any matter other than a Bill, the Speaker must rule on whether the matter affects or does not affect counties. (3) When the Senate votes on a matter that does not affect counties, each senator has one vote.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu decisions of Seneti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) On election, all the members of the Senate who were registered as voters in a particular county must collectively constitute a single delegation for purposes of clause (4) and the member elected under Article 98(1)(a) must be the head of the delegation. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Decisions of Senate, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) When the Senate is to vote on any matter other than a Bill, the Speaker must rule on whether the matter affects or does not affect counties. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Decisions of Senate, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu decisions of Seneti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu decisions of Seneti.

articles.title: Committees and Standing Orders
articles.simplified: (1) Each House of Parliament can establish committees, and must make Standing Orders for the orderly conduct of its proceedings, including the proceedings of its committees. (2) Parliament can establish joint committees consisting of members of both Houses and can jointly regulate the procedure of those committees. (3) The proceedings of either House are not invalid just because of— (a) a vacancy in its membership; or (b) the presence or participation of You not entitled to be present at, or to participate in, the proceedings of the House.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Each House of Parliament can establish committees, and must make Standing Orders for the orderly conduct of its proceedings, including the proceedings of its committees. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Committees and Standing Orders, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament can establish joint committees consisting of members of both Houses and can jointly regulate the procedure of those committees. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Committees and Standing Orders, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Power to call for evidence
articles.simplified: (1) Either House of Parliament, and any of its committees, has power to summon You to appear before it for the purpose of giving evidence or providing information. (2) For the purposes of clause (1), a House of Parliament and any of its committees has the same powers as the High Court— (a) to enforce the attendance of witnesses and examine them on oath, affirmation or otherwise; (b) to compel the production of documents; and (c) to issue a commission or request to examine witnesses abroad.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu power to call for evidence. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Power to call for evidence, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) For the purposes of clause (1), a House of Parliament and any of its committees has the same powers as the High Court— (a) to enforce the attendance of witnesses and examine them on oath, affirmation or otherwise; (b) to compel the production of documents; and (c) to issue a commission or request to examine witnesses abroad. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Power to call for evidence, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Location of sittings of Parliament
articles.simplified: (1) A sitting of either House can be held at any place within Kenya and can commence at any time that the House appoints. (2) Whenever a new House is elected, the President, by notice in the Gazette, must appoint the place and date for the first sitting of the new House, which must be not more than thirty days after the election.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu location of sittings of Bunge. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A sitting of either House can be held at any place within Kenya and can commence at any time that the House appoints. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Location of sittings of Parliament, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Whenever a new House is elected, the President, by notice in the Gazette, must appoint the place and date for the first sitting of the new House, which must be not more than thirty days after the election. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Location of sittings of Parliament, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu location of sittings of Bunge.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu location of sittings of Bunge.

articles.title: Parliamentary Service Commission
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the Parliamentary Service Commission. (2) The Commission consists of— (a) the Speaker of the National Assembly, as chairperson; (b) a vice-chairperson elected by the Commission from the members appointed under paragraph (c); (c) seven members appointed by Parliament from among its members of whom— (i) four must be nominated equally from both Houses by the party or coalition of parties forming the national government, of whom at least two must be women; and (ii) three must be nominated by the parties not forming the national government, at least one of whom must be nominated from each House and at least one of whom must be a woman; and (d) one man and one woman appointed by Parliament from among persons who are experienced in public affairs, but are not members of Parliament. (3) The Clerk of the Senate must be the Secretary to the Commission.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu tume ya Huduma za Bunge. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the Parliamentary Service Commission. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Parliamentary Service Commission, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Commission consists of— (a) the Speaker of the National Assembly, as chairperson; (b) a vice-chairperson elected by the Commission from the members appointed under paragraph (c); (c) seven members appointed by Parliament from among its members of whom— (i) four must be nominated equally from both Houses by the party or coalition of parties forming the national government, of whom at least two must be women; and (ii) three must be nominated by the parties not forming the national government, at least one of whom must be nominated from each House and at least one of whom must be a woman; and (d) one man and one woman appointed by Parliament from among persons who are experienced in public affairs, but are not members of Parliament. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Parliamentary Service Commission, that is how it should be handled.

Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu tume ya Huduma za Bunge.

articles.title: Clerks and staff of Parliament
articles.simplified: (1) There must be a Clerk for each House of Parliament, appointed by the Parliamentary Service Commission with the approval of the relevant House. (2) The offices of the Clerks and offices of members of the staff of the Clerks must be offices in the Parliamentary Service.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There must be a Clerk for each House of Parliament, appointed by the Parliamentary Service Commission with the approval of the relevant House. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Clerks and staff of Parliament, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The offices of the Clerks and offices of members of the staff of the Clerks must be offices in the Parliamentary Service. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Clerks and staff of Parliament, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Power
articles.simplified: Executive authority is exercised for the service of the people.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Executive authority derives from the people of Kenya and must be exercised in accordance with this Constitution. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Principles of executive authority, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Executive authority must be exercised in a manner compatible with the principle of service to the people of Kenya, and for your well-being and benefit. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Principles of executive authority, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: The National Executive
articles.simplified: (1) The national executive of the Republic comprises the President, the Deputy President and the rest of the Cabinet. (2) The composition of the national executive must reflect the regional and ethnic diversity of the people of Kenya.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The national executive of the Republic comprises the President, the Deputy President and the rest of the Cabinet. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on The National Executive, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The composition of the national executive must reflect the regional and ethnic diversity of the people of Kenya. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on The National Executive, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: President
articles.simplified: The President is the symbol of national unity and Head of State.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The President— (a) is the Head of State and Government; (b) exercises the executive authority of the Republic, with the assistance of the Deputy President and Cabinet Secretaries; (c) is the Commander-in-Chief of the Kenya Defence Forces; (d) is the chairperson of the National Security Council; and (e) is a symbol of national unity. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Authority of the President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The President must— (a) respect, uphold and safeguard this Constitution; (b) safeguard the sovereignty of the Republic; (c) promote and enhance the unity of the nation; (d) promote respect for the diversity of the people and communities of Kenya; and (e) ensure the protection of human rights and fundamental freedoms and the rule of law. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Authority of the President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Functions of the President
articles.simplified: (1) The President must— (a) address the opening of each newly elected Parliament; (b) address a special sitting of Parliament once every year and can address Parliament at any other time; and (c) once every year— (i) report, in an address to the nation, on all the measures taken and the progress achieved in the realisation of the national values, referred to in Article 10; (ii) publish in the Gazette the details of the measures and progress under sub-paragraph (i); and (iii) submit a report for debate to the National Assembly on the progress made in fulfilling the international obligations of the Republic. (2) The President must nominate and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appoint, and can dismiss— (a) the Cabinet Secretaries, in accordance with Article 152; (b) the Attorney-General, in accordance with Article 156; (c) the Secretary to the Cabinet in accordance with Article 154; (d) Principal Secretaries in accordance with Article 155; (e) high commissioners, ambassadors and diplomatic and consular representatives; and (f) in accordance with this Constitution, any other State or public officer whom this Constitution requires or empowers the President to appoint or dismiss. (3) The President must— (a) chair Cabinet meetings; (b) direct and co-ordinate the functions of ministries and government departments; and (c) by a decision published in the Gazette, assign responsibility for the implementation and administration of any Act of Parliament to a Cabinet Secretary, to the extent not inconsistent with any Act of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The President must— (a) address the opening of each newly elected Parliament; (b) address a special sitting of Parliament once every year and can address Parliament at any other time; and (c) once every year— (i) report, in an address to the nation, on all the measures taken and the progress achieved in the realisation of the national values, referred to in Article 10; (ii) publish in the Gazette the details of the measures and progress under sub-paragraph (i); and (iii) submit a report for debate to the National Assembly on the progress made in fulfilling the international obligations of the Republic. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Functions of the President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The President must nominate and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appoint, and can dismiss— (a) the Cabinet Secretaries, in accordance with Article 152; (b) the Attorney-General, in accordance with Article 156; (c) the Secretary to the Cabinet in accordance with Article 154; (d) Principal Secretaries in accordance with Article 155; (e) high commissioners, ambassadors and diplomatic and consular representatives; and (f) in accordance with this Constitution, any other State or public officer whom this Constitution requires or empowers the President to appoint or dismiss. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Functions of the President, that is how it should be handled.

Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Power of mercy
articles.simplified: (1) On the petition of You, the President can exercise a power of mercy in accordance with the advice of the Advisory Committee established under clause (2), by— (a) granting a free or conditional pardon to You convicted of an offence; (b) postponing the carrying out of a punishment, either for a specified or indefinite period; (c) substituting a less severe form of punishment; or (d) remitting all or part of a punishment. (2) There must be an Advisory Committee on the Power of Mercy, comprising— (a) the Attorney-General; (b) the Cabinet Secretary responsible for correctional services; and (c) at least five other members as prescribed by an Act of Parliament, none of whom can be a State officer or in public service. (3) Parliament must enact legislation to provide for— (a) the tenure of the members of the Advisory Committee; (b) the procedure of the Advisory Committee; and (c) criteria that must be applied by the Advisory Committee in formulating its advice.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu power of mercy. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) On the petition of You, the President can exercise a power of mercy in accordance with the advice of the Advisory Committee established under clause (2), by— (a) granting a free or conditional pardon to You convicted of an offence; (b) postponing the carrying out of a punishment, either for a specified or indefinite period; (c) substituting a less severe form of punishment; or (d) remitting all or part of a punishment. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Power of mercy, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) There must be an Advisory Committee on the Power of Mercy, comprising— (a) the Attorney-General; (b) the Cabinet Secretary responsible for correctional services; and (c) at least five other members as prescribed by an Act of Parliament, none of whom can be a State officer or in public service. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Power of mercy, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu power of mercy.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu power of mercy.

articles.title: Exercise of presidential powers during temporary incumbency
articles.swTitle: Exercise of raisial mamlakas during temporary incumbency
articles.simplified: (1) You who holds the office of President or who is authorised in terms of this Constitution to exercise the powers of the President— (a) during the period commencing on the date of the first vote in a presidential election, and ending when the newly elected President assumes office; or (b) while the President is absent or incapacitated, or at other times contemplated in Article 147(3), can not exercise the powers of the President specified in clause (2). (2) The powers referred to in clause (1) are— (a) the nomination or appointment of the judges of the superior courts; (b) the nomination or appointment of any other public officer whom this Constitution or legislation requires the President to appoint; (c) the nomination or appointment or dismissal of Cabinet Secretaries and other State or Public officers; (d) the nomination or appointment or dismissal of a high commissioner, ambassador, or diplomatic or consular representative; (e) the power of mercy; and (f) the authority to confer honours in the name of the people and the Republic.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu exercise of raisial mamlakas during temporary incumbency. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) You who holds the office of President or who is authorised in terms of this Constitution to exercise the powers of the President— (a) during the period commencing on the date of the first vote in a presidential election, and ending when the newly elected President assumes office; or (b) while the President is absent or incapacitated, or at other times contemplated in Article 147(3), can not exercise the powers of the President specified in clause (2). That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Exercise of presidential powers during temporary incumbency, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The powers referred to in clause (1) are— (a) the nomination or appointment of the judges of the superior courts; (b) the nomination or appointment of any other public officer whom this Constitution or legislation requires the President to appoint; (c) the nomination or appointment or dismissal of Cabinet Secretaries and other State or Public officers; (d) the nomination or appointment or dismissal of a high commissioner, ambassador, or diplomatic or consular representative; (e) the power of mercy; and (f) the authority to confer honours in the name of the people and the Republic. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Exercise of presidential powers during temporary incumbency, that is how it should be handled.

Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu exercise of raisial mamlakas during temporary incumbency.

articles.title: Decisions of the President
articles.simplified: A decision of the President in the performance of any function of the President under this Constitution must be in writing and must bear the seal and signature of the President. It also guides what officials must or must not do for you.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: A decision of the President in the performance of any function of the President under this Constitution must be in writing and must bear the seal and signature of the President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Decisions of the President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: Decisions of the President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Decisions of the President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Election of the President
articles.simplified: (1) The President must be elected by registered voters in a national election conducted in accordance with this Constitution and any Act of Parliament regulating presidential elections. (2) An election of the President must be held— (a) on the same day as a general election of Members of Parliament, being the second Tuesday in August, in every fifth year; or (b) in the circumstances contemplated in Article 146.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The President must be elected by registered voters in a national election conducted in accordance with this Constitution and any Act of Parliament regulating presidential elections. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Election of the President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) An election of the President must be held— (a) on the same day as a general election of Members of Parliament, being the second Tuesday in August, in every fifth year; or (b) in the circumstances contemplated in Article 146. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Election of the President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Qualifications and disqualifications for election as President
articles.simplified: (1) You qualifies for nomination as a presidential candidate if the person— (a) is a citizen by birth; (b) is qualified to stand for election as a member of Parliament; (c) is nominated by a political party, or is an independent candidate; and (d) is nominated by not fewer than two thousand voters from each of a majority of the counties. (2) You is not qualified for nomination as a presidential candidate if the person— (a) owes allegiance to a foreign state; or (b) is a public officer, or is acting in any State or other public office. (3) Clause (2)(b) must not apply to— (a) the President; (b) the Deputy President; or (c) a member of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Qualifications and disqualifications for election as President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) You is not qualified for nomination as a presidential candidate if the person— (a) owes allegiance to a foreign state; or (b) is a public officer, or is acting in any State or other public office. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Qualifications and disqualifications for election as President, that is how it should be handled.
