Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Procedure at presidential election
articles.simplified: (1) If only one candidate for President is nominated, that candidate must be declared elected. (2) If two or more candidates for President are nominated, an election must be held in each constituency. (3) In a presidential election— (a) all persons registered as voters for the purposes of parliamentary elections are entitled to vote; (b) the poll must be taken by secret ballot on the day specified in Article 101(1) at the time, in the places and in the manner prescribed under an Act of Parliament; and (c) after counting the votes in the polling stations, the Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission must tally and verify the count and declare the result.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu procedure at raisial uchaguzi. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) If only one candidate for President is nominated, that candidate must be declared elected. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Procedure at presidential election, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If two or more candidates for President are nominated, an election must be held in each constituency. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Procedure at presidential election, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu procedure at raisial uchaguzi.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu procedure at raisial uchaguzi.

articles.title: Death before assuming office
articles.simplified: (1) If a President-elect dies after being declared elected as President, but before assuming office— (a) the Deputy President-elect must be sworn in as acting President on the date on which the President-elect would otherwise have been sworn-in; and (b) a fresh election to the office of President must be held within sixty days after the death of the President-elect. (2) If the Deputy President-elect dies before assuming office, the office of the Deputy President must be declared vacant on the assumption of office by the person declared elected as the President. (3) If both the persons declared elected as the President and the Deputy President die before assuming office— (a) the Speaker of the National Assembly must act as President from the date on which the President-elect would otherwise have been sworn-in; and (b) a fresh presidential election must be conducted within sixty days after the second death.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu death before assuming ofisi. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) If a President-elect dies after being declared elected as President, but before assuming office— (a) the Deputy President-elect must be sworn in as acting President on the date on which the President-elect would otherwise have been sworn-in; and (b) a fresh election to the office of President must be held within sixty days after the death of the President-elect. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Death before assuming office, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If the Deputy President-elect dies before assuming office, the office of the Deputy President must be declared vacant on the assumption of office by the person declared elected as the President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Death before assuming office, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu death before assuming ofisi.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu death before assuming ofisi.

articles.title: Questions as to validity of presidential election
articles.swTitle: Questions as to validity of raisial uchaguzi
articles.simplified: (1) You can file a petition in the Supreme Court to challenge the election of the President-elect within seven days after the date of the declaration of the results of the presidential election. (2) Within fourteen days after the filing of a petition under clause (1), the Supreme Court must hear and determine the petition and its decision must be final. (3) If the Supreme Court determines the election of the President-elect to be invalid, a fresh election must be held within sixty days after the determination.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu questions as to validity of raisial uchaguzi. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) You can file a petition in the Supreme Court to challenge the election of the President-elect within seven days after the date of the declaration of the results of the presidential election. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Questions as to validity of presidential election, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Within fourteen days after the filing of a petition under clause (1), the Supreme Court must hear and determine the petition and its decision must be final. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Questions as to validity of presidential election, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu questions as to validity of raisial uchaguzi.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu questions as to validity of raisial uchaguzi.

articles.title: Assumption of office of President
articles.simplified: (1) The swearing in of the President-elect must be in public before the Chief Justice, or, in the absence of the Chief Justice, the Deputy Chief Justice. (2) The President-elect must be sworn in on the first Tuesday following— (a) the fourteenth day after the date of the declaration of the result of the presidential election, if no petition has been filed under Article 140; or (b) the seventh day following the date on which the court renders a decision declaring the election to be valid, if any petition has been filed under Article 140. (3) The President-elect assumes office by taking and subscribing to the oath or affirmation of allegiance, and the oath or affirmation for the execution of the functions of office, as prescribed in the Third Schedule.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu assumption of ofisi of Rais. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The swearing in of the President-elect must be in public before the Chief Justice, or, in the absence of the Chief Justice, the Deputy Chief Justice. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Assumption of office of President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The President-elect must be sworn in on the first Tuesday following— (a) the fourteenth day after the date of the declaration of the result of the presidential election, if no petition has been filed under Article 140; or (b) the seventh day following the date on which the court renders a decision declaring the election to be valid, if any petition has been filed under Article 140. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Assumption of office of President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu assumption of ofisi of Rais.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu assumption of ofisi of Rais.

articles.title: Term Limits
articles.simplified: The President shall hold office for a term of five years, max two terms.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu term of ofisi of Rais. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The President must hold office for a term beginning on the date on which the President was sworn in, and ending when the person next elected President in accordance with Article 136(2)(a) is sworn in. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Term of office of President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) You must not hold office as President for more than two terms. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Term of office of President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu term of ofisi of Rais.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu term of ofisi of Rais.

articles.title: Immunity
articles.simplified: President cannot be sued in court during their term of office.
articles.swSimplified: Rais cannot be sued in mahakama during their term of ofisi.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Criminal proceedings must not be instituted or continued in any court against the President or You performing the functions of that office, during your tenure of office. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Protection from legal proceedings, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Civil proceedings must not be instituted in any court against the President or the person performing the functions of that office during your tenure of office in respect of anything done or not done in the exercise of your powers under this Constitution. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Protection from legal proceedings, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu protection from legal proceedings.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu protection from legal proceedings.

articles.title: Removal of President on grounds of incapacity
articles.swTitle: Removal of Rais on grounds of incapacity
articles.simplified: (1) A member of the National Assembly, supported by at least a quarter of all the members, can move a motion for the investigation of the President’s physical or mental capacity to perform the functions of office. (2) If a motion under clause (1) is supported by a majority of all the members of the National Assembly— (a) the Speaker must inform the Chief Justice of that resolution within two days; and (b) the President must continue to perform the functions of the office pending the outcome of the proceedings required by this Article. (3) Within seven days after receiving notice of the resolution from the Speaker, the Chief Justice must appoint a tribunal consisting of— (a) three persons who are qualified to practise medicine under the laws of Kenya, nominated by the body which by law is responsible for regulating the professional practice of medicine; (b) one advocate of the High Court nominated by the body which by law is responsible for regulating the professional practice of advocates; and (c) one person nominated by the President.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu removal of Rais on grounds of incapacity. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A member of the National Assembly, supported by at least a quarter of all the members, can move a motion for the investigation of the President’s physical or mental capacity to perform the functions of office. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal of President on grounds of incapacity, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If a motion under clause (1) is supported by a majority of all the members of the National Assembly— (a) the Speaker must inform the Chief Justice of that resolution within two days; and (b) the President must continue to perform the functions of the office pending the outcome of the proceedings required by this Article. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal of President on grounds of incapacity, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu removal of Rais on grounds of incapacity.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu removal of Rais on grounds of incapacity.

articles.title: Removal of President by impeachment
articles.simplified: (1) A member of the National Assembly, supported by at least a third of all the members, can move a motion for the impeachment of the President— (a) on the ground of a gross violation of a provision of this Constitution or of any other law; (b) where there are serious reasons for believing that the President has committed a crime under national or international law; or (c) for gross misconduct. (2) If a motion under clause (1) is supported by at least two-thirds of all the members of the National Assembly— (a) the Speaker must inform the Speaker of the Senate of that resolution within two days; and (b) the President must continue to perform the functions of the office pending the outcome of the proceedings required by this Article. (3) Within seven days after receiving notice of a resolution from the Speaker of the National Assembly— (a) the Speaker of the Senate must convene a meeting of the Senate to hear charges against the President; and (b) the Senate, by resolution, can appoint a special committee comprising eleven of its members to investigate the matter.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu removal of Rais by impeachment. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A member of the National Assembly, supported by at least a third of all the members, can move a motion for the impeachment of the President— (a) on the ground of a gross violation of a provision of this Constitution or of any other law; (b) where there are serious reasons for believing that the President has committed a crime under national or international law; or (c) for gross misconduct. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal of President by impeachment, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If a motion under clause (1) is supported by at least two-thirds of all the members of the National Assembly— (a) the Speaker must inform the Speaker of the Senate of that resolution within two days; and (b) the President must continue to perform the functions of the office pending the outcome of the proceedings required by this Article. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal of President by impeachment, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu removal of Rais by impeachment.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu removal of Rais by impeachment.

articles.title: Vacancy in the office of President
articles.simplified: (1) The office of President must become vacant if the holder of the office — (a) dies; (b) resigns, in writing, addressed to the Speaker of the National Assembly; or (c) otherwise ceases to hold office under Article 144 or 145 or under any other provision of this Constitution. (2) When a vacancy occurs in the office of President— (a) the Deputy President must assume office as President for the remainder of the term of the President; or (b) if the office of Deputy President is vacant, or the Deputy President is unable to assume the office of President, the Speaker of the National Assembly must act as President and an election to the office of President must be held within sixty days after the vacancy arose in the office of President. (3) You who assumes the office of President under clause (2)(a), or following an election required by clause (2)(b), must, unless otherwise removed from office under this Constitution, hold office until a newly elected President is sworn in following the next regularly scheduled election under Article 136(2)(a).
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The office of President must become vacant if the holder of the office — (a) dies; (b) resigns, in writing, addressed to the Speaker of the National Assembly; or (c) otherwise ceases to hold office under Article 144 or 145 or under any other provision of this Constitution. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Vacancy in the office of President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) When a vacancy occurs in the office of President— (a) the Deputy President must assume office as President for the remainder of the term of the President; or (b) if the office of Deputy President is vacant, or the Deputy President is unable to assume the office of President, the Speaker of the National Assembly must act as President and an election to the office of President must be held within sixty days after the vacancy arose in the office of President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Vacancy in the office of President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Functions of the Deputy President
articles.simplified: (1) The Deputy President must be the principal assistant of the President and must deputise for the President in the execution of the President’s functions. (2) The Deputy President must perform the functions conferred by this Constitution and any other functions of the President as the President can assign. (3) Subject to Article 134, when the President is absent or is temporarily incapacitated, and during any other period that the President decides, the Deputy President must act as the President.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The Deputy President must be the principal assistant of the President and must deputise for the President in the execution of the President’s functions. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Functions of the Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Deputy President must perform the functions conferred by this Constitution and any other functions of the President as the President can assign. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Functions of the Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Election and swearing-in of Deputy President
articles.simplified: (1) Each candidate in a presidential election must nominate You who is qualified for nomination for election as President, as a candidate for Deputy President. (2) For the purposes of clause (1), there must be no separate nomination process for the Deputy President and Article 137(1)(d) must not apply to a candidate for Deputy President. (3) The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission must declare the candidate nominated by the person who is elected as the President to be elected as the Deputy President.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Each candidate in a presidential election must nominate You who is qualified for nomination for election as President, as a candidate for Deputy President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Election and swearing-in of Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) For the purposes of clause (1), there must be no separate nomination process for the Deputy President and Article 137(1)(d) must not apply to a candidate for Deputy President. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Election and swearing-in of Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Vacancy in the office of Deputy President
articles.simplified: (1) Within fourteen days after a vacancy in the office of Deputy President arises, the President must nominate You to fill the vacancy, and the National Assembly must vote on the nomination within sixty days after receiving it. (2) If You assumes office as Deputy President under clause (1), then, for the purposes of Article 148(8), the person must be deemed— (a) to have served a full term as Deputy President if, at the date on which the person assumed office, more than two and a half years remain before the date of the next regularly scheduled election under Article 136(2)(a); or (b) not to have served a term of office as Deputy President, in any other case.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Within fourteen days after a vacancy in the office of Deputy President arises, the President must nominate You to fill the vacancy, and the National Assembly must vote on the nomination within sixty days after receiving it. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Vacancy in the office of Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If You assumes office as Deputy President under clause (1), then, for the purposes of Article 148(8), the person must be deemed— (a) to have served a full term as Deputy President if, at the date on which the person assumed office, more than two and a half years remain before the date of the next regularly scheduled election under Article 136(2)(a); or (b) not to have served a term of office as Deputy President, in any other case. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Vacancy in the office of Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Removal of Deputy President
articles.simplified: (1) The Deputy President can be removed from office— (a) on the ground of physical or mental incapacity to perform the functions of the office; or (b) on impeachment— (i) on the ground of a gross violation of a provision of this Constitution or any other law; (ii) where there are serious reasons to believe that the Deputy President has committed a crime under national or international law; or (iii) for gross misconduct. (2) The provisions of Articles 144 and 145 relating to the removal of the President must apply, with the necessary modifications, to the removal of the Deputy President.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu removal of Naibu wa Rais. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The Deputy President can be removed from office— (a) on the ground of physical or mental incapacity to perform the functions of the office; or (b) on impeachment— (i) on the ground of a gross violation of a provision of this Constitution or any other law; (ii) where there are serious reasons to believe that the Deputy President has committed a crime under national or international law; or (iii) for gross misconduct. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal of Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The provisions of Articles 144 and 145 relating to the removal of the President must apply, with the necessary modifications, to the removal of the Deputy President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal of Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu removal of Naibu wa Rais.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu removal of Naibu wa Rais.

articles.title: Remuneration and benefits of President and Deputy President
articles.simplified: (1) The remuneration and benefits payable to the President and the Deputy President must be a charge on the Consolidated Fund. (2) The remuneration, benefits and privileges of the President and Deputy President must not be varied to your disadvantage while in office. (3) The retirement benefits payable to a former President and a former Deputy President, the facilities available to and the privileges enjoyed by them, must not be varied to your disadvantage during your lifetime.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The remuneration and benefits payable to the President and the Deputy President must be a charge on the Consolidated Fund. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Remuneration and benefits of President and Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The remuneration, benefits and privileges of the President and Deputy President must not be varied to your disadvantage while in office. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Remuneration and benefits of President and Deputy President, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Cabinet
articles.simplified: (1) The Cabinet consists of— (a) the President; (b) the Deputy President; (c) the Attorney-General; and (d) not fewer than fourteen and not more than twenty-two Cabinet Secretaries. (2) The President must nominate and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appoint Cabinet Secretaries. (3) A Cabinet Secretary must not be a Member of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu baraza la Mawaziri. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The Cabinet consists of— (a) the President; (b) the Deputy President; (c) the Attorney-General; and (d) not fewer than fourteen and not more than twenty-two Cabinet Secretaries. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Cabinet, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The President must nominate and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appoint Cabinet Secretaries. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Cabinet, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu baraza la Mawaziri.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu baraza la Mawaziri.

articles.title: Decisions, responsibility and accountability of the Cabinet
articles.simplified: (1) A decision by the Cabinet must be in writing. (2) Cabinet Secretaries are accountable individually, and collectively, to the President for the exercise of your powers and the performance of your functions. (3) A Cabinet Secretary must attend before a committee of the National Assembly, or the Senate, when required by the committee, and answer any question concerning a matter for which the Cabinet Secretary is responsible.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A decision by the Cabinet must be in writing. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Decisions, responsibility and accountability of the Cabinet, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Cabinet Secretaries are accountable individually, and collectively, to the President for the exercise of your powers and the performance of your functions. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Decisions, responsibility and accountability of the Cabinet, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Secretary to the Cabinet
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the office of Secretary to the Cabinet, which is an office in the public service. (2) The Secretary to the Cabinet must— (a) be nominated and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appointed by the President; and (b) can be dismissed by the President. (3) The Secretary to the Cabinet must— (a) have charge of the Cabinet office; (b) be responsible, subject to the directions of the Cabinet, for arranging the business, and keeping the minutes, of the Cabinet; (c) convey the decisions of the Cabinet to the appropriate persons or authorities; and (d) have other functions as directed by the Cabinet.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the office of Secretary to the Cabinet, which is an office in the public service. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Secretary to the Cabinet, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Secretary to the Cabinet must— (a) be nominated and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appointed by the President; and (b) can be dismissed by the President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Secretary to the Cabinet, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Principal Secretaries
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the office of Principal Secretary, which is an office in the public service. (2) Each State department must be under the administration of a Principal Secretary. (3) The President must— (a) nominate You for appointment as Principal Secretary from among persons recommended by the Public Service Commission; and (b) with the approval of the National Assembly, appoint Principal Secretaries.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu principal Secretaries. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the office of Principal Secretary, which is an office in the public service. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Principal Secretaries, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Each State department must be under the administration of a Principal Secretary. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Principal Secretaries, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu principal Secretaries.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu principal Secretaries.

articles.title: Attorney-General
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the office of Attorney-General. (2) The Attorney-General must be nominated by the President and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appointed by the President. (3) The qualifications for appointment as Attorney-General are the same as for appointment to the office of Chief Justice.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu mwanasheria Mkuu. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the office of Attorney-General. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Attorney-General, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Attorney-General must be nominated by the President and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appointed by the President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Attorney-General, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu mwanasheria Mkuu.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu mwanasheria Mkuu.

articles.title: Director of Public Prosecutions
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the office of Director of Public Prosecutions. (2) The Director of Public Prosecutions must be nominated and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appointed by the President. (3) The qualifications for appointment as Director of Public Prosecutions are the same as for the appointment as a judge of the High Court.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu mkurugenzi wa Mashtaka ya Umma. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the office of Director of Public Prosecutions. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Director of Public Prosecutions, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Director of Public Prosecutions must be nominated and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appointed by the President. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Director of Public Prosecutions, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu mkurugenzi wa Mashtaka ya Umma.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu mkurugenzi wa Mashtaka ya Umma.

articles.title: Removal and resignation of Director of Public Prosecutions
articles.simplified: (1) The Director of Public Prosecutions can be removed from office only on the grounds of— (a) inability to perform the functions of office arising from mental or physical incapacity; (b) non-compliance with Chapter Six; (c) bankruptcy; (d) incompetence; or (e) gross misconduct or misbehaviour. (2) You desiring the removal of the Director of Public Prosecutions can present a petition to the Public Service Commission which, must be in writing, setting out the alleged facts constituting the grounds for the removal of the Director. (3) The Public Service Commission must consider the petition and, if it is satisfied that it discloses the existence of a ground under clause (1), it must send the petition to the President.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The Director of Public Prosecutions can be removed from office only on the grounds of— (a) inability to perform the functions of office arising from mental or physical incapacity; (b) non-compliance with Chapter Six; (c) bankruptcy; (d) incompetence; or (e) gross misconduct or misbehaviour. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal and resignation of Director of Public Prosecutions, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) You desiring the removal of the Director of Public Prosecutions can present a petition to the Public Service Commission which, must be in writing, setting out the alleged facts constituting the grounds for the removal of the Director. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal and resignation of Director of Public Prosecutions, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Courts
articles.simplified: Justice shall be done to all and not delayed.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Judicial authority is derived from the people and vests in, and must be exercised by, the courts and tribunals established by or under this Constitution. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Judicial authority, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) In exercising judicial authority, the courts and tribunals must be guided by the following principles— (a) justice must be done to all, irrespective of status; (b) justice must not be delayed; (c) alternative forms of dispute resolution including reconciliation, mediation, arbitration and traditional dispute resolution mechanisms must be promoted, subject to clause (3); (d) justice must be administered without undue regard to procedural technicalities; and (e) the purpose and principles of this Constitution must be protected and promoted. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Judicial authority, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Independence of the Judiciary
articles.simplified: (1) In the exercise of judicial authority, the Judiciary, as constituted by Article 161, must be subject only to this Constitution and the law and must not be subject to the control or direction of You or authority. (2) The office of a judge of a superior court must not be abolished while there is a substantive holder of the office. (3) The remuneration and benefits payable to or in respect of judges must be a charge on the Consolidated Fund.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) In the exercise of judicial authority, the Judiciary, as constituted by Article 161, must be subject only to this Constitution and the law and must not be subject to the control or direction of You or authority. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Independence of the Judiciary, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The office of a judge of a superior court must not be abolished while there is a substantive holder of the office. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Independence of the Judiciary, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Judicial offices and officers
articles.simplified: (1) The Judiciary consists of the judges of the superior courts, magistrates, other judicial officers and staff. (2) There is established the office of— (a) Chief Justice, who must be the Head of the Judiciary; (b) Deputy Chief Justice, who must be the Deputy Head of the Judiciary; and (c) Chief Registrar of the Judiciary, who must be the chief administrator and accounting officer of the Judiciary. (3) The Judicial Service Commission can establish other offices of registrar as can be necessary.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The Judiciary consists of the judges of the superior courts, magistrates, other judicial officers and staff. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Judicial offices and officers, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) There is established the office of— (a) Chief Justice, who must be the Head of the Judiciary; (b) Deputy Chief Justice, who must be the Deputy Head of the Judiciary; and (c) Chief Registrar of the Judiciary, who must be the chief administrator and accounting officer of the Judiciary. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Judicial offices and officers, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: System of courts
articles.simplified: (1) The superior courts are the Supreme Court, the Court of Appeal, the High Court and the courts referred to in clause (2). (2) Parliament must establish courts with the status of the High Court to hear and determine disputes relating to— (a) employment and labour relations; and (b) the environment and the use and occupation of, and title to, land. (3) Parliament must determine the jurisdiction and functions of the courts contemplated in clause (2).
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu system of mahakama. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The superior courts are the Supreme Court, the Court of Appeal, the High Court and the courts referred to in clause (2). That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on System of courts, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament must establish courts with the status of the High Court to hear and determine disputes relating to— (a) employment and labour relations; and (b) the environment and the use and occupation of, and title to, land. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on System of courts, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu system of mahakama.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu system of mahakama.

articles.title: Supreme
articles.simplified: Highest court in Kenya, final arbiter of law.
articles.swSimplified: Highest mahakama in Kenya, final arbiter of sheria.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the Supreme Court, which must consist of— (a) the Chief Justice, who must be the president of the court; (b) the Deputy Chief Justice, who must— (i) deputise for the Chief Justice; and (ii) be the vice-president of the court; and (c) five other judges. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Supreme Court, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Supreme Court must be properly constituted for the purposes of its proceedings if it is composed of five judges. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Supreme Court, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu mahakama ya Juu.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu mahakama ya Juu.

articles.title: Court of Appeal
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the Court of Appeal, which— (a) must consist of the number of judges, being not fewer than twelve, as can be prescribed by an Act of Parliament; and (b) must be organised and administered in the manner prescribed by an Act of Parliament. (2) There must be a president of the Court of Appeal who must be elected by the judges of the Court of Appeal from among themselves. (3) The Court of Appeal has jurisdiction to hear appeals from— (a) the High Court; and (b) any other court or tribunal as prescribed by an Act of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu mahakama ya Rufaa. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the Court of Appeal, which— (a) must consist of the number of judges, being not fewer than twelve, as can be prescribed by an Act of Parliament; and (b) must be organised and administered in the manner prescribed by an Act of Parliament. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Court of Appeal, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) There must be a president of the Court of Appeal who must be elected by the judges of the Court of Appeal from among themselves. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Court of Appeal, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu mahakama ya Rufaa.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu mahakama ya Rufaa.

articles.title: High Court
articles.simplified: Has the power to decide if any law is unconstitutional.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu mahakama Kuu. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the High Court, which— (a) must consist of the number of judges prescribed by an Act of Parliament; and (b) must be organised and administered in the manner prescribed by an Act of Parliament. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on High Court, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) There must be a Principal Judge of the High Court, who must be elected by the judges of the High Court from among themselves. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on High Court, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu mahakama Kuu.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu mahakama Kuu.

articles.title: Appointment of Chief Justice, Deputy Chief Justice and other judges
articles.simplified: (1) The President must appoint— (a) the Chief Justice and the Deputy Chief Justice, in accordance with the recommendation of the Judicial Service Commission, and subject to the approval of the National Assembly; and (b) all other judges, in accordance with the recommendation of the Judicial Service Commission. (2) Each judge of a superior court must be appointed from among persons who— (a) hold a law degree from a recognised university, or are advocates of the High Court of Kenya, or possess an equivalent qualification in a common-law jurisdiction; (b) possess the experience required under clause (3) to (5) as applicable, irrespective of whether that experience was gained in Kenya or in another Commonwealth common-law jurisdiction; and (c) have a high moral character, integrity and impartiality. (3) The Chief Justice and other judges of the Supreme Court must be appointed from among persons who have— (a) at least fifteen years’ experience as a superior court judge; or (b) at least fifteen years’ experience as a distinguished academic, judicial officer, legal practitioner or such experience in other relevant legal field; or (c) held the qualifications specified in paragraphs (a) and (b) for a period amounting, in the aggregate, to fifteen years.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The President must appoint— (a) the Chief Justice and the Deputy Chief Justice, in accordance with the recommendation of the Judicial Service Commission, and subject to the approval of the National Assembly; and (b) all other judges, in accordance with the recommendation of the Judicial Service Commission. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Appointment of Chief Justice, Deputy Chief Justice and other judges, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Each judge of a superior court must be appointed from among persons who— (a) hold a law degree from a recognised university, or are advocates of the High Court of Kenya, or possess an equivalent qualification in a common-law jurisdiction; (b) possess the experience required under clause (3) to (5) as applicable, irrespective of whether that experience was gained in Kenya or in another Commonwealth common-law jurisdiction; and (c) have a high moral character, integrity and impartiality. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Appointment of Chief Justice, Deputy Chief Justice and other judges, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Tenure of office of the Chief Justice and other judges
articles.simplified: (1) A judge must retire from office on attaining the age of seventy years, but can elect to retire at any time after attaining the age of sixty-five years. (2) The Chief Justice must hold office for a maximum of ten years or until retiring under clause (1), whichever is the earlier. (3) If the Chief Justice’s term of office expires before the Chief Justice retires under clause (1), the Chief Justice can continue in office as a judge of the Supreme Court.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A judge must retire from office on attaining the age of seventy years, but can elect to retire at any time after attaining the age of sixty-five years. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Tenure of office of the Chief Justice and other judges, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Chief Justice must hold office for a maximum of ten years or until retiring under clause (1), whichever is the earlier. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Tenure of office of the Chief Justice and other judges, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Removal from office
articles.simplified: (1) A judge of a superior court can be removed from office only on the grounds of— (a) inability to perform the functions of office arising from mental or physical incapacity; (b) a breach of a code of conduct prescribed for judges of the superior courts by an Act of Parliament; (c) bankruptcy; (d) incompetence; or (e) gross misconduct or misbehaviour. (2) The removal of a judge can be initiated only by the Judicial Service Commission acting on its own motion, or on the petition of You to the Judicial Service Commission. (3) A petition by You to the Judicial Service Commission under clause (2) must be in writing, setting out the alleged facts constituting the grounds for the judge's removal.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu removal from ofisi. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A judge of a superior court can be removed from office only on the grounds of— (a) inability to perform the functions of office arising from mental or physical incapacity; (b) a breach of a code of conduct prescribed for judges of the superior courts by an Act of Parliament; (c) bankruptcy; (d) incompetence; or (e) gross misconduct or misbehaviour. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal from office, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The removal of a judge can be initiated only by the Judicial Service Commission acting on its own motion, or on the petition of You to the Judicial Service Commission. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal from office, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu removal from ofisi.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu removal from ofisi.

articles.title: Subordinate courts
articles.simplified: (1) The subordinate courts are— (a) the Magistrates' courts; (b) the Kadhis’ courts; (c) the Courts Martial; and (d) any other court or local tribunal as can be established by an Act of Parliament, other than the courts established as required by Article 162(2). (2) Parliament must enact legislation conferring jurisdiction, functions and powers on the courts established under clause (1).
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu subordinate mahakama. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The subordinate courts are— (a) the Magistrates' courts; (b) the Kadhis’ courts; (c) the Courts Martial; and (d) any other court or local tribunal as can be established by an Act of Parliament, other than the courts established as required by Article 162(2). That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Subordinate courts, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament must enact legislation conferring jurisdiction, functions and powers on the courts established under clause (1). That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Subordinate courts, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu subordinate mahakama.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu subordinate mahakama.

articles.title: Kadhis’ courts
articles.simplified: (1) There must be a Chief Kadhi and such number, being not fewer than three, of other Kadhis as can be prescribed under an Act of Parliament. (2) You must not be qualified to be appointed to hold or act in the office of Kadhi unless the person— (a) professes the Muslim religion; and (b) possesses such knowledge of the Muslim law applicable to any sects of Muslims as qualifies the person, in the opinion of the Judicial Service Commission, to hold a Kadhi’s court. (3) Parliament must establish Kadhis’ courts, each of which must have the jurisdiction and powers conferred on it by legislation, subject to clause (5).
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu kadhis’ mahakama. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There must be a Chief Kadhi and such number, being not fewer than three, of other Kadhis as can be prescribed under an Act of Parliament. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Kadhis’ courts, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) You must not be qualified to be appointed to hold or act in the office of Kadhi unless the person— (a) professes the Muslim religion; and (b) possesses such knowledge of the Muslim law applicable to any sects of Muslims as qualifies the person, in the opinion of the Judicial Service Commission, to hold a Kadhi’s court. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Kadhis’ courts, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu kadhis’ mahakama.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu kadhis’ mahakama.

articles.title: JSC
articles.simplified: Judicial Service Commission manages judges and courts.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the Judicial Service Commission. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Establishment of the Judicial Service Commission, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Commission must consist of— (a) the Chief Justice, who must be the chairperson of the Commission; (b) one Supreme Court judge elected by the judges of the Supreme Court; (c) one Court of Appeal judge elected by the judges of the Court of Appeal; (d) one High Court judge and one magistrate, one a woman and one a man, elected by the members of the association of judges and magistrates; (e) the Attorney-General; (f) two advocates, one a woman and one a man, each of whom has at least fifteen years’ experience, elected by the members of the statutory body responsible for the professional regulation of advocates; (g) one person nominated by the Public Service Commission; and (h) one woman and one man to represent the public, not being lawyers, appointed by the President with the approval of the National Assembly. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Establishment of the Judicial Service Commission, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Functions of the Judicial Service Commission
articles.simplified: (1) The Judicial Service Commission must promote and facilitate the independence and accountability of the judiciary and the efficient, effective and transparent administration of justice and must— (a) recommend to the President persons for appointment as judges; (b) review and make recommendations on the conditions of service of— (i) judges and judicial officers, other than your remuneration; and (ii) the staff of the Judiciary; (c) appoint, receive complaints against, investigate and remove from office or otherwise discipline registrars, magistrates, other judicial officers and other staff of the Judiciary, in the manner prescribed by an Act of Parliament; (d) prepare and implement programmes for the continuing education and training of judges and judicial officers; and (e) advise the national government on improving the efficiency of the administration of justice. (2) In the performance of its functions, the Commission must be guided by the following— (a) competitiveness and transparent processes of appointment of judicial officers and other staff of the judiciary; and (b) the promotion of gender equality.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The Judicial Service Commission must promote and facilitate the independence and accountability of the judiciary and the efficient, effective and transparent administration of justice and must— (a) recommend to the President persons for appointment as judges; (b) review and make recommendations on the conditions of service of— (i) judges and judicial officers, other than your remuneration; and (ii) the staff of the Judiciary; (c) appoint, receive complaints against, investigate and remove from office or otherwise discipline registrars, magistrates, other judicial officers and other staff of the Judiciary, in the manner prescribed by an Act of Parliament; (d) prepare and implement programmes for the continuing education and training of judges and judicial officers; and (e) advise the national government on improving the efficiency of the administration of justice. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Functions of the Judicial Service Commission, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) In the performance of its functions, the Commission must be guided by the following— (a) competitiveness and transparent processes of appointment of judicial officers and other staff of the judiciary; and (b) the promotion of gender equality. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Functions of the Judicial Service Commission, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Judiciary Fund
articles.simplified: (1) There is established a fund to be known as the Judiciary Fund which must be administered by the Chief Registrar of the Judiciary. (2) The Fund must be used for administrative expenses of the Judiciary and such other purposes as can be necessary for the discharge of the functions of the Judiciary. (3) Each financial year, the Chief Registrar must prepare estimates of expenditure for the following year, and submit them to the National Assembly for approval.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu judiciary Fund. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established a fund to be known as the Judiciary Fund which must be administered by the Chief Registrar of the Judiciary. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Judiciary Fund, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The Fund must be used for administrative expenses of the Judiciary and such other purposes as can be necessary for the discharge of the functions of the Judiciary. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Judiciary Fund, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu judiciary Fund.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu judiciary Fund.

articles.title: Devolve
articles.simplified: Aims to promote democracy and accountable power.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu objects of devolution. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: The objects of the devolution of government are— (a) to promote democratic and accountable exercise of power; (b) to foster national unity by recognising diversity; (c) to give powers of self-governance to the people and enhance the participation of the people in the exercise of the powers of the State and in making decisions affecting them; (d) to recognise the right of communities to manage your own affairs and to further your development; (e) to protect and promote the interests and rights of minorities and marginalised communities; (f) to promote social and economic development and the provision of proximate, easily accessible services throughout Kenya; (g) to ensure equitable sharing of national and local resources throughout Kenya; (h) to facilitate the decentralisation of State organs, your functions and services, from the capital of Kenya; and (i) to enhance checks and balances and the separation of powers. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Objects of devolution, that is how it should be handled.
Achieng is in a real situation: Objects of devolution. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Objects of devolution, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu objects of devolution.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu objects of devolution.

articles.title: Principles of devolved government
articles.simplified: County governments established under this Constitution must reflect the following principles— (a) county governments must be based on democratic principles and the separation of powers; (b) county governments must have reliable sources of revenue to enable them to govern and deliver services effectively; and (c) no more than two-thirds of the members of representative bodies in each county government must be of the same gender. It also guides what officials must or must not do for you.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu principles of devolved serikali. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: County governments established under this Constitution must reflect the following principles— (a) county governments must be based on democratic principles and the separation of powers; (b) county governments must have reliable sources of revenue to enable them to govern and deliver services effectively; and (c) no more than two-thirds of the members of representative bodies in each county government must be of the same gender. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Principles of devolved government, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: Principles of devolved government. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Principles of devolved government, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu principles of devolved serikali.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu principles of devolved serikali.

articles.title: County governments
articles.simplified: (1) There must be a county government for each county, consisting of a county assembly and a county executive. (2) Every county government must decentralise its functions and the provision of its services to the extent that it is efficient and practicable to do so.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu kaunti serikalis. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There must be a county government for each county, consisting of a county assembly and a county executive. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on County governments, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Every county government must decentralise its functions and the provision of its services to the extent that it is efficient and practicable to do so. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on County governments, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu kaunti serikalis.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu kaunti serikalis.

articles.title: Membership of county assembly
articles.simplified: (1) A county assembly consists of— (a) members elected by the registered voters of the wards, each ward constituting a single member constituency, on the same day as a general election of Members of Parliament, being the second Tuesday in August, in every fifth year; (b) the number of special seat members necessary to ensure that no more than two-thirds of the membership of the assembly are of the same gender; (c) the number of members of marginalised groups, including persons with disabilities and the youth, prescribed by an Act of Parliament; and (d) the Speaker, who is an ex officio member. (2) The members contemplated in clause (1)(b) and (c) must, in each case, be nominated by political parties in proportion to the seats received in that election in that county by each political party under paragraph (a) in accordance with Article 90. (3) The filling of special seats under clause (1)(b) must be determined after declaration of elected members from each ward.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu membership of bunge la kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A county assembly consists of— (a) members elected by the registered voters of the wards, each ward constituting a single member constituency, on the same day as a general election of Members of Parliament, being the second Tuesday in August, in every fifth year; (b) the number of special seat members necessary to ensure that no more than two-thirds of the membership of the assembly are of the same gender; (c) the number of members of marginalised groups, including persons with disabilities and the youth, prescribed by an Act of Parliament; and (d) the Speaker, who is an ex officio member. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Membership of county assembly, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Speaker of a county assembly
articles.swTitle: Speaker of a bunge la kaunti
articles.simplified: (1) Each county assembly must have a speaker elected by the county assembly from among persons who are not members of the assembly. (2) A sitting of the county assembly must be presided over by— (a) the speaker of the assembly; or (b) in the absence of the speaker, another member of the assembly elected by the assembly. (3) Parliament must enact legislation providing for the election and removal from office of speakers of the county assemblies.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu speaker of a bunge la kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Each county assembly must have a speaker elected by the county assembly from among persons who are not members of the assembly. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Speaker of a county assembly, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) A sitting of the county assembly must be presided over by— (a) the speaker of the assembly; or (b) in the absence of the speaker, another member of the assembly elected by the assembly. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Speaker of a county assembly, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu speaker of a bunge la kaunti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu speaker of a bunge la kaunti.

articles.title: County Executive
articles.simplified: Led by the Governor and the deputy governor.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu kaunti executive committees. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The executive authority of the county is vested in, and exercised by, a county executive committee. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on County executive committees, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The county executive committee consists of— (a) the county governor and the deputy county governor; and (b) members appointed by the county governor, with the approval of the assembly, from among persons who are not members of the assembly. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on County executive committees, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu kaunti executive committees.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu kaunti executive committees.

articles.title: Governor
articles.simplified: Elected directly by county voters for 5 years.
articles.swSimplified: Elected directly by kaunti kurars for 5 years.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The county governor must be directly elected by the voters registered in the county, on the same day as a general election of Members of Parliament, being the second Tuesday in August, in every fifth year. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Election of county governor and deputy county governor, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) To be eligible for election as county governor, You must be eligible for election as a member of the county assembly. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Election of county governor and deputy county governor, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Removal of a county governor
articles.swTitle: Removal of a gavana wa kaunti
articles.simplified: (1) A county governor can be removed from office on any of the following grounds— (a) gross violation of this Constitution or any other law; (b) where there are serious reasons for believing that the county governor has committed a crime under national or international law; (c) abuse of office or gross misconduct; or (d) physical or mental incapacity to perform the functions of office of county governor. (2) Parliament must enact legislation providing for the procedure of removal of a county governor on any of the grounds specified in clause (1).
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu removal of a gavana wa kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A county governor can be removed from office on any of the following grounds— (a) gross violation of this Constitution or any other law; (b) where there are serious reasons for believing that the county governor has committed a crime under national or international law; (c) abuse of office or gross misconduct; or (d) physical or mental incapacity to perform the functions of office of county governor. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal of a county governor, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament must enact legislation providing for the procedure of removal of a county governor on any of the grounds specified in clause (1). That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Removal of a county governor, that is how it should be handled.
