articles.title: Vacancy in the office of county governor
articles.simplified: (1) The office of the county governor must become vacant if the holder of the office— (a) dies; (b) resigns, in writing, addressed to the speaker of the county assembly; (c) ceases to be eligible to be elected county governor under Article 180(2); (d) is convicted of an offence punishable by imprisonment for at least twelve months; or (e) is removed from office under this Constitution. (2) If a vacancy occurs in the office of county governor, the deputy county governor must assume office as county governor for the remainder of the term of the county governor. (3) If You assumes office as county governor under clause (2), the person must be deemed for the purposes of Article 180(7)— (a) to have served a full term as county governor if, at the date on which the person assumed office, more than two and a half years remain before the date of the next regularly scheduled election under Article 180(1); or (b) not to have served a term of office as county governor, in any other case.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The office of the county governor must become vacant if the holder of the office— (a) dies; (b) resigns, in writing, addressed to the speaker of the county assembly; (c) ceases to be eligible to be elected county governor under Article 180(2); (d) is convicted of an offence punishable by imprisonment for at least twelve months; or (e) is removed from office under this Constitution. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Vacancy in the office of county governor, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If a vacancy occurs in the office of county governor, the deputy county governor must assume office as county governor for the remainder of the term of the county governor. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Vacancy in the office of county governor, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Functions of county executive committees
articles.simplified: (1) A county executive committee must— (a) implement county legislation; (b) implement, within the county, national legislation to the extent that the legislation so requires; (c) manage and coordinate the functions of the county administration and its departments; and (d) perform any other functions conferred on it by this Constitution or national legislation. (2) A county executive committee can prepare proposed legislation for consideration by the county assembly. (3) The county executive committee must provide the county assembly with full and regular reports on matters relating to the county.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu functions of kaunti executive committees. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A county executive committee must— (a) implement county legislation; (b) implement, within the county, national legislation to the extent that the legislation so requires; (c) manage and coordinate the functions of the county administration and its departments; and (d) perform any other functions conferred on it by this Constitution or national legislation. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Functions of county executive committees, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) A county executive committee can prepare proposed legislation for consideration by the county assembly. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Functions of county executive committees, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu functions of kaunti executive committees.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu functions of kaunti executive committees.

articles.title: Urban areas and cities
articles.simplified: (1) National legislation must provide for the governance and management of urban areas and cities and must, in particular— (a) establish criteria for classifying areas as urban areas and cities; (b) establish the principles of governance and management of urban areas and cities; and (c) provide for participation by residents in the governance of urban areas and cities. (2) National legislation contemplated in clause (1) can include mechanisms for identifying different categories of urban areas and cities, and for your governance.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) National legislation must provide for the governance and management of urban areas and cities and must, in particular— (a) establish criteria for classifying areas as urban areas and cities; (b) establish the principles of governance and management of urban areas and cities; and (c) provide for participation by residents in the governance of urban areas and cities. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Urban areas and cities, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) National legislation contemplated in clause (1) can include mechanisms for identifying different categories of urban areas and cities, and for your governance. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Urban areas and cities, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Legislative authority of county assemblies
articles.simplified: (1) The legislative authority of a county is vested in, and exercised by, its county assembly. (2) A county assembly can make any laws that are necessary for, or incidental to, the effective performance of the functions and exercise of the powers of the county government under the Fourth Schedule. (3) A county assembly, while respecting the principle of the separation of powers, can exercise oversight over the county executive committee and any other county executive organs.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The legislative authority of a county is vested in, and exercised by, its county assembly. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Legislative authority of county assemblies, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) A county assembly can make any laws that are necessary for, or incidental to, the effective performance of the functions and exercise of the powers of the county government under the Fourth Schedule. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Legislative authority of county assemblies, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Powers
articles.simplified: Division of functions between national and county governments.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Except as otherwise provided by this Constitution, the functions and powers of the national government and the county governments, respectively, are as set out in the Fourth Schedule. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Respective functions and powers of national and county governments, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) A function or power that is conferred on more than one level of government is a function or power within the concurrent jurisdiction of each of those levels of government. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Respective functions and powers of national and county governments, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Transfer of functions and powers between levels of government
articles.simplified: (1) A function or power of government at one level can be transferred to a government at the other level by agreement between the governments if— (a) the function or power would be more effectively performed or exercised by the receiving government; and (b) the transfer of the function or power is not prohibited by the legislation under which it is to be performed or exercised. (2) If a function or power is transferred from a government at one level to a government at the other level— (a) arrangements must be put in place to ensure that the resources necessary for the performance of the function or exercise of the power are transferred; and (b) constitutional responsibility for the performance of the function or exercise of the power must remain with the government to which it is assigned by the Fourth Schedule.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A function or power of government at one level can be transferred to a government at the other level by agreement between the governments if— (a) the function or power would be more effectively performed or exercised by the receiving government; and (b) the transfer of the function or power is not prohibited by the legislation under which it is to be performed or exercised. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Transfer of functions and powers between levels of government, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If a function or power is transferred from a government at one level to a government at the other level— (a) arrangements must be put in place to ensure that the resources necessary for the performance of the function or exercise of the power are transferred; and (b) constitutional responsibility for the performance of the function or exercise of the power must remain with the government to which it is assigned by the Fourth Schedule. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Transfer of functions and powers between levels of government, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Boundaries of counties
articles.simplified: (1) The boundaries of a county can be altered only by a resolution— (a) recommended by an independent commission set up for that purpose by Parliament; and (b) passed by— (i) the National Assembly, with the support of at least two-thirds of all of the members of the Assembly; and (ii) the Senate, with the support of at least two-thirds of all of the county delegations. (2) The boundaries of a county can be altered to take into account— (a) population density and demographic trends; (b) physical and human infrastructure; (c) historical and cultural ties; (d) the cost of administration; (e) the views of the communities affected; (f) the objects of devolution of government; and (g) geographical features.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu boundaries of counties. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The boundaries of a county can be altered only by a resolution— (a) recommended by an independent commission set up for that purpose by Parliament; and (b) passed by— (i) the National Assembly, with the support of at least two-thirds of all of the members of the Assembly; and (ii) the Senate, with the support of at least two-thirds of all of the county delegations. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Boundaries of counties, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The boundaries of a county can be altered to take into account— (a) population density and demographic trends; (b) physical and human infrastructure; (c) historical and cultural ties; (d) the cost of administration; (e) the views of the communities affected; (f) the objects of devolution of government; and (g) geographical features. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Boundaries of counties, that is how it should be handled.

Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu boundaries of counties.

articles.title: Cooperation between national and county governments
articles.simplified: (1) Government at either level must— (a) perform its functions, and exercise its powers, in a manner that respects the functional and institutional integrity of government at the other level, and respects the constitutional status and institutions of government at the other level and, in the case of county government, within the county level; (b) assist, support and consult and, as appropriate, implement the legislation of the other level of government; and (c) liaise with government at the other level for the purpose of exchanging information, coordinating policies and administration and enhancing capacity. (2) Government at each level, and different governments at the county level, must co-operate in the performance of functions and exercise of powers and, for that purpose, can set up joint committees and joint authorities. (3) In any dispute between governments, the governments must make every reasonable effort to settle the dispute, including by means of procedures provided under national legislation.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Government at either level must— (a) perform its functions, and exercise its powers, in a manner that respects the functional and institutional integrity of government at the other level, and respects the constitutional status and institutions of government at the other level and, in the case of county government, within the county level; (b) assist, support and consult and, as appropriate, implement the legislation of the other level of government; and (c) liaise with government at the other level for the purpose of exchanging information, coordinating policies and administration and enhancing capacity. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Cooperation between national and county governments, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Government at each level, and different governments at the county level, must co-operate in the performance of functions and exercise of powers and, for that purpose, can set up joint committees and joint authorities. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Cooperation between national and county governments, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Support for county governments
articles.simplified: (1) Parliament must by legislation ensure that county governments have adequate support to enable them to perform your functions. (2) County governments must operate financial management systems that comply with any requirements prescribed by national legislation. (3) Parliament must, by legislation, provide for intervention by the national government if a county government— (a) is unable to perform its functions; or (b) does not operate a financial management system that complies with the requirements prescribed by national legislation.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu support for serikali za kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Parliament must by legislation ensure that county governments have adequate support to enable them to perform your functions. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Support for county governments, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) County governments must operate financial management systems that comply with any requirements prescribed by national legislation. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Support for county governments, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu support for serikali za kaunti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu support for serikali za kaunti.

articles.title: Conflict of laws
articles.simplified: (1) This Article applies to conflicts between national and county legislation in respect of matters falling within the concurrent jurisdiction of both levels of government. (2) National legislation prevails over county legislation if— (a) the national legislation applies uniformly throughout Kenya and any of the conditions specified in clause (3) is satisfied; or (b) the national legislation is aimed at preventing unreasonable action by a county that— (i) is prejudicial to the economic, health or security interests of Kenya or another county; or (ii) impedes the implementation of national economic policy. (3) The following are the conditions referred to in clause (2)(a)— (a) the national legislation provides for a matter that cannot be regulated effectively by legislation enacted by the individual counties; (b) the national legislation provides for a matter that, to be dealt with effectively, requires uniformity across the nation, and the national legislation provides that uniformity by establishing— (i) norms and standards; or (ii) national policies; or (c) the national legislation is necessary for— (i) the maintenance of national security; (ii) the maintenance of economic unity; (iii) the protection of the common market in respect of the mobility of goods, services, capital and labour; (iv) the promotion of economic activities across county boundaries; (v) the promotion of equal opportunity or equal access to government services; or (vi) the protection of the environment.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu conflict of sheria. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) This Article applies to conflicts between national and county legislation in respect of matters falling within the concurrent jurisdiction of both levels of government. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Conflict of laws, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) National legislation prevails over county legislation if— (a) the national legislation applies uniformly throughout Kenya and any of the conditions specified in clause (3) is satisfied; or (b) the national legislation is aimed at preventing unreasonable action by a county that— (i) is prejudicial to the economic, health or security interests of Kenya or another county; or (ii) impedes the implementation of national economic policy. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Conflict of laws, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu conflict of sheria.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu conflict of sheria.

articles.title: Suspension of a county government
articles.swTitle: Suspension of a serikali ya kaunti
articles.simplified: (1) The President can suspend a county government— (a) in an emergency arising out of internal conflict or war; or (b) in any other exceptional circumstances. (2) A county government must not be suspended under clause (1)(b) unless an independent commission of inquiry has investigated allegations against the county government, the President is satisfied that the allegations are justified and the Senate has authorised the suspension. (3) During a suspension under this Article, arrangements must be made for the performance of the functions of a county government in accordance with an Act of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu suspension of a serikali ya kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The President can suspend a county government— (a) in an emergency arising out of internal conflict or war; or (b) in any other exceptional circumstances. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Suspension of a county government, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) A county government must not be suspended under clause (1)(b) unless an independent commission of inquiry has investigated allegations against the county government, the President is satisfied that the allegations are justified and the Senate has authorised the suspension. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Suspension of a county government, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu suspension of a serikali ya kaunti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu suspension of a serikali ya kaunti.

articles.title: Qualifications for election as member of county assembly
articles.swTitle: Qualifications for uchaguzi as member of bunge la kaunti
articles.simplified: (1) Unless disqualified under clause (2), You is eligible for election as a member of a county assembly if the person— (a) is registered as a voter; (b) satisfies any educational, moral and ethical requirements prescribed by this Constitution or an Act of Parliament; and (c) is either— (i) nominated by a political party; or (ii) an independent candidate supported by at least five hundred registered voters in the ward concerned. (2) You is disqualified from being elected a member of a county assembly if the person— (a) is a State officer or other public officer, other than a member of the county assembly; (b) has, at any time within the five years immediately before the date of election, held office as a member of the Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission; (c) has not been a citizen of Kenya for at least the ten years immediately preceding the date of election; (d) is of unsound mind; (e) is an undischarged bankrupt; (f) is serving a sentence of imprisonment of at least six months; or (g) has been found, in accordance with any law, to have misused or abused a State office or public office or to have contravened Chapter Six. (3) You is not disqualified under clause (2) unless all possibility of appeal or review of the relevant sentence or decision has been exhausted.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu qualifications for uchaguzi as member of bunge la kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Unless disqualified under clause (2), You is eligible for election as a member of a county assembly if the person— (a) is registered as a voter; (b) satisfies any educational, moral and ethical requirements prescribed by this Constitution or an Act of Parliament; and (c) is either— (i) nominated by a political party; or (ii) an independent candidate supported by at least five hundred registered voters in the ward concerned. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Qualifications for election as member of county assembly, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) You is disqualified from being elected a member of a county assembly if the person— (a) is a State officer or other public officer, other than a member of the county assembly; (b) has, at any time within the five years immediately before the date of election, held office as a member of the Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission; (c) has not been a citizen of Kenya for at least the ten years immediately preceding the date of election; (d) is of unsound mind; (e) is an undischarged bankrupt; (f) is serving a sentence of imprisonment of at least six months; or (g) has been found, in accordance with any law, to have misused or abused a State office or public office or to have contravened Chapter Six. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Qualifications for election as member of county assembly, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu qualifications for uchaguzi as member of bunge la kaunti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu qualifications for uchaguzi as member of bunge la kaunti.

articles.title: Vacation of office of member of county assembly
articles.swTitle: Vacation of ofisi of member of bunge la kaunti
articles.simplified: (1) The office of a member of a county assembly becomes vacant— (a) if the member dies; (b) if the member is absent from eight sittings of the assembly without permission, in writing, of the speaker of the assembly, and is unable to offer satisfactory explanation for the absence; (c) if the member is removed from office under this Constitution or legislation enacted under Article 80; (d) if the member resigns in writing addressed to the speaker of the assembly; (e) if, having been elected to the assembly— (i) as a member of a political party, the member resigns from the party, or is deemed to have resigned from the party as determined in accordance with the legislation contemplated in clause (2); or (ii) as an independent candidate, the member joins a political party; (f) at the end of the term of the assembly; or (g) if the member becomes disqualified for election on grounds specified in Article 193(2). (2) Parliament must enact legislation providing for the circumstances under which a member of a political party must be deemed, for the purposes of clause (1)(e), to have resigned from the party.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu vacation of ofisi of member of bunge la kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The office of a member of a county assembly becomes vacant— (a) if the member dies; (b) if the member is absent from eight sittings of the assembly without permission, in writing, of the speaker of the assembly, and is unable to offer satisfactory explanation for the absence; (c) if the member is removed from office under this Constitution or legislation enacted under Article 80; (d) if the member resigns in writing addressed to the speaker of the assembly; (e) if, having been elected to the assembly— (i) as a member of a political party, the member resigns from the party, or is deemed to have resigned from the party as determined in accordance with the legislation contemplated in clause (2); or (ii) as an independent candidate, the member joins a political party; (f) at the end of the term of the assembly; or (g) if the member becomes disqualified for election on grounds specified in Article 193(2). That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Vacation of office of member of county assembly, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament must enact legislation providing for the circumstances under which a member of a political party must be deemed, for the purposes of clause (1)(e), to have resigned from the party. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Vacation of office of member of county assembly, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu vacation of ofisi of member of bunge la kaunti.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu vacation of ofisi of member of bunge la kaunti.

articles.title: County assembly power to summon witnesses
articles.swTitle: Kaunti assembly mamlaka to summon witnesses
articles.simplified: (1) A county assembly or any of its committees has power to summon You to appear before it for the purpose of giving evidence or providing information. (2) For the purposes of clause (1), an assembly has the same powers as the High Court to— (a) enforce the attendance of witnesses and examining them on oath, affirmation or otherwise; (b) compel the production of documents; and (c) issue a commission or request to examine witnesses abroad.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu kaunti assembly mamlaka to summon witnesses. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) A county assembly or any of its committees has power to summon You to appear before it for the purpose of giving evidence or providing information. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on County assembly power to summon witnesses, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) For the purposes of clause (1), an assembly has the same powers as the High Court to— (a) enforce the attendance of witnesses and examining them on oath, affirmation or otherwise; (b) compel the production of documents; and (c) issue a commission or request to examine witnesses abroad. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on County assembly power to summon witnesses, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu kaunti assembly mamlaka to summon witnesses.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu kaunti assembly mamlaka to summon witnesses.

articles.title: Public participation and county assembly powers, privileges and immunities
articles.simplified: (1) A county assembly must— (a) conduct its business in an open manner, and hold its sittings and those of its committees, in public; and (b) facilitate public participation and involvement in the legislative and other business of the assembly and its committees. (2) A county assembly can not exclude the public, or any media, from any sitting unless in exceptional circumstances the speaker has determined that there are justifiable reasons for doing so. (3) Parliament must enact legislation providing for the powers, privileges and immunities of county assemblies, your committees and members.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Public participation and county assembly powers, privileges and immunities, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) A county assembly can not exclude the public, or any media, from any sitting unless in exceptional circumstances the speaker has determined that there are justifiable reasons for doing so. articles.title: County assembly gender balance and diversity
articles.simplified: (1) Not more than two-thirds of the members of any county assembly or county executive committee must be of the same gender. (2) Parliament must enact legislation to— (a) ensure that the community and cultural diversity of a county is reflected in its county assembly and county executive committee; and (b) prescribe mechanisms to protect minorities within counties.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on County assembly gender balance and diversity, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Parliament must enact legislation to— (a) ensure that the community and cultural diversity of a county is reflected in its county assembly and county executive committee; and (b) prescribe mechanisms to protect minorities within counties. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on County assembly gender balance and diversity, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: County government during transition
articles.simplified: While an election is being held to constitute a county assembly under this Chapter, the executive committee of the county, as last constituted remains competent to perform administrative functions until a new executive committee is constituted after the election. It also guides what officials must or must not do for you.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu kaunti serikali during transition. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: While an election is being held to constitute a county assembly under this Chapter, the executive committee of the county, as last constituted remains competent to perform administrative functions until a new executive committee is constituted after the election. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on County government during transition, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: County government during transition. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on County government during transition, that is how it should be handled.

Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu kaunti serikali during transition.

articles.title: Publication of county legislation
articles.simplified: (1) County legislation does not take effect unless published in the Gazette. (2) National and county legislation can prescribe additional requirements in respect of the publication of county legislation.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) National and county legislation can prescribe additional requirements in respect of the publication of county legislation. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Publication of county legislation, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Legislation on Chapter
articles.simplified: (1) Parliament must enact legislation providing for all matters necessary or convenient to give effect to this Chapter. (2) In particular, provision can be made with respect to— (a) the governance of the capital city, other cities and urban areas; (b) the transfer of functions and powers by one level of government to another, including the transfer of legislative powers from the national government to county governments; (c) the manner of election or appointment of persons to, and your removal from, offices in county governments, including the qualifications of voters and candidates; (d) the procedure of assemblies and executive committees including the chairing and frequency of meetings, quorums and voting; and (e) the suspension of assemblies and executive committees.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Parliament must enact legislation providing for all matters necessary or convenient to give effect to this Chapter. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Legislation on Chapter, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) In particular, provision can be made with respect to— (a) the governance of the capital city, other cities and urban areas; (b) the transfer of functions and powers by one level of government to another, including the transfer of legislative powers from the national government to county governments; (c) the manner of election or appointment of persons to, and your removal from, offices in county governments, including the qualifications of voters and candidates; (d) the procedure of assemblies and executive committees including the chairing and frequency of meetings, quorums and voting; and (e) the suspension of assemblies and executive committees. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Legislation on Chapter, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Taxes
articles.simplified: Burden of taxation shared fairly across generations.
articles.swSimplified: Burden of kodiation kugawanywa kwa haki across generations.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: The following principles must guide all aspects of public finance in the Republic— (a) there must be openness and accountability, including public participation in financial matters; (b) the public finance system must promote an equitable society, and in particular— (i) the burden of taxation must be shared fairly; (ii) revenue raised nationally must be shared equitably among national and county governments; and (iii) expenditure must promote the equitable development of the country, including by making special provision for marginalised groups and areas; (c) the burden and benefits of the use of resources and public borrowing must be shared equitably between present and future generations; (d) public money must be used in a prudent and responsible way; and (e) financial management must be responsible, and fiscal reporting must be clear. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Principles of public finance, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: Principles of public finance. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Principles of public finance, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu principles of public finance.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu principles of public finance.

articles.title: Equitable sharing of national revenue
articles.simplified: (1) Revenue raised nationally must be shared equitably among the national and county governments. (2) County governments can be given additional allocations from the national government’s share of the revenue, either conditionally or unconditionally.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu equitable sharing of national revenue. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Revenue raised nationally must be shared equitably among the national and county governments. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Equitable sharing of national revenue, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) County governments can be given additional allocations from the national government’s share of the revenue, either conditionally or unconditionally. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Equitable sharing of national revenue, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu equitable sharing of national revenue.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu equitable sharing of national revenue.

exampleSources.title: Understanding the Access to Information law

articles.title: Revenue Sharing
articles.simplified: At least 15% of national revenue must go to counties.
articles.swSimplified: At least 15% of national revenue lazima go to counties.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The following criteria must be taken into account in determining the equitable shares provided for under Article 202 and in all national legislation concerning county government enacted in terms of this Chapter— (a) the national interest; (b) any provision that must be made in respect of the public debt and other national obligations; (c) the needs of the national government, determined by objective criteria; (d) the need to ensure that county governments are able to perform the functions allocated to them; (e) the fiscal capacity and efficiency of county governments; (f) developmental and other needs of counties; (g) economic disparities within and among counties and the need to remedy them; (h) the need for affirmative action in respect of disadvantaged areas and groups; (i) the need for economic optimisation of each county and to provide incentives for each county to optimise its capacity to raise revenue; (j) the desirability of stable and predictable allocations of revenue; and (k) the need for flexibility in responding to emergencies and other temporary needs, based on similar objective criteria. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Equitable share and other financial laws, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) For every financial year, the equitable share of the revenue raised nationally that is allocated to county governments must be not less than fifteen per cent of all revenue collected by the national government. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Equitable share and other financial laws, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

articles.title: Equalisation Fund
articles.simplified: (1) There is established an Equalisation Fund into which must be paid one half per cent of all the revenue collected by the national government each year calculated on the basis of the most recent audited accounts of revenue received, as approved by the National Assembly. (2) The national government must use the Equalisation Fund only to provide basic services including water, roads, health facilities and electricity to marginalised areas to the extent necessary to bring the quality of those services in those areas to the level generally enjoyed by the rest of the nation, so far as possible. (3) The national government can use the Equalisation Fund— (a) only to the extent that the expenditure of those funds has been approved in an Appropriation Bill enacted by Parliament; and (b) either directly, or indirectly through conditional grants to counties in which marginalised communities exist.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu equalisation Fund. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established an Equalisation Fund into which must be paid one half per cent of all the revenue collected by the national government each year calculated on the basis of the most recent audited accounts of revenue received, as approved by the National Assembly. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Equalisation Fund, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The national government must use the Equalisation Fund only to provide basic services including water, roads, health facilities and electricity to marginalised areas to the extent necessary to bring the quality of those services in those areas to the level generally enjoyed by the rest of the nation, so far as possible. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Equalisation Fund, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu equalisation Fund.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu equalisation Fund.

articles.title: Consultation on financial legislation affecting counties
articles.simplified: (1) When a Bill that includes provisions dealing with the sharing of revenue, or any financial matter concerning county governments is published, the Commission on Revenue Allocation must consider those provisions and can make recommendations to the National Assembly and the Senate. (2) Any recommendations made by the Commission must be tabled in Parliament, and each House must consider the recommendations before voting on the Bill.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Consultation on financial legislation affecting counties, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Any recommendations made by the Commission must be tabled in Parliament, and each House must consider the recommendations before voting on the Bill. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Consultation on financial legislation affecting counties, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Consolidated Fund and other public funds
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the Consolidated Fund into which must be paid all money raised or received by or on behalf of the national government, except money that— (a) is reasonably excluded from the Fund by an Act of Parliament and payable into another public fund established for a specific purpose; or (b) can, under an Act of Parliament, be retained by the State organ that received it for the purpose of defraying the expenses of the State organ. (2) Money can be withdrawn from the Consolidated Fund only— (a) in accordance with an appropriation by an Act of Parliament; (b) in accordance with Article 222 or 223; or (c) as a charge against the Fund as authorised by this Constitution or an Act of Parliament. (3) Money must not be withdrawn from any national public fund other than the Consolidated Fund, unless the withdrawal of the money has been authorised by an Act of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There is established the Consolidated Fund into which must be paid all money raised or received by or on behalf of the national government, except money that— (a) is reasonably excluded from the Fund by an Act of Parliament and payable into another public fund established for a specific purpose; or (b) can, under an Act of Parliament, be retained by the State organ that received it for the purpose of defraying the expenses of the State organ. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Consolidated Fund and other public funds, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Money can be withdrawn from the Consolidated Fund only— (a) in accordance with an appropriation by an Act of Parliament; (b) in accordance with Article 222 or 223; or (c) as a charge against the Fund as authorised by this Constitution or an Act of Parliament. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Consolidated Fund and other public funds, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Revenue Funds for county governments
articles.swTitle: Revenue Funds for serikali za kaunti
articles.simplified: (1) There must be established a Revenue Fund for each county government, into which must be paid all money raised or received by or on behalf of the county government, except money reasonably excluded by an Act of Parliament. (2) Money can be withdrawn from the Revenue Fund of a county government only— (a) as a charge against the Revenue Fund that is provided for by an Act of Parliament or by legislation of the county; or (b) as authorised by an appropriation by legislation of the county. (3) Money must not be withdrawn from a Revenue Fund unless the Controller of Budget has approved the withdrawal.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu revenue Funds for serikali za kaunti. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) There must be established a Revenue Fund for each county government, into which must be paid all money raised or received by or on behalf of the county government, except money reasonably excluded by an Act of Parliament. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Revenue Funds for county governments, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Money can be withdrawn from the Revenue Fund of a county government only— (a) as a charge against the Revenue Fund that is provided for by an Act of Parliament or by legislation of the county; or (b) as authorised by an appropriation by legislation of the county. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Revenue Funds for county governments, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Contingencies Fund
articles.simplified: (1) There is established a Contingencies Fund, the operation of which must be in accordance with an Act of Parliament. (2) An Act of Parliament must provide for advances from the Contingencies Fund if the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance is satisfied that there is an urgent and unforeseen need for expenditure for which there is no other authority.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu contingencies Fund. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Contingencies Fund, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Power to impose taxes and charges
articles.simplified: (1) Only the national government can impose— (a) income tax; (b) value-added tax; (c) customs duties and other duties on import and export goods; and (d) excise tax. (2) An Act of Parliament can authorise the national government to impose any other tax or duty, except a tax specified in clause (3)(a) or (b). (3) A county can impose— (a) property rates; (b) entertainment taxes; and (c) any other tax that it is authorised to impose by an Act of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Only the national government can impose— (a) income tax; (b) value-added tax; (c) customs duties and other duties on import and export goods; and (d) excise tax. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Power to impose taxes and charges, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) An Act of Parliament can authorise the national government to impose any other tax or duty, except a tax specified in clause (3)(a) or (b). That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Power to impose taxes and charges, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu ibara hii.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu ibara hii.

exampleSources.title: Understanding the Access to Information law

articles.title: Imposition of tax
articles.simplified: (1) No tax or licensing fee can be imposed, waived or varied except as provided by legislation. (2) If legislation permits the waiver of any tax or licensing fee— (a) a public record of each waiver must be maintained together with the reason for the waiver; and (b) each waiver, and the reason for it, must be reported to the Auditor-General. (3) No law can exclude or authorise the exclusion of a State officer from payment of tax by reason of— (a) the office held by that State officer; or (b) the nature of the work of the State officer.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu imposition of kodi. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) No tax or licensing fee can be imposed, waived or varied except as provided by legislation. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Imposition of tax, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) If legislation permits the waiver of any tax or licensing fee— (a) a public record of each waiver must be maintained together with the reason for the waiver; and (b) each waiver, and the reason for it, must be reported to the Auditor-General. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Imposition of tax, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu imposition of kodi.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu imposition of kodi.

articles.title: Borrowing by national government
articles.simplified: (1) Parliament can, by legislation— (a) prescribe the terms on which the national government can borrow; and (b) impose reporting requirements. (2) Within seven days after either House of Parliament so requests by resolution, the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance must present to the relevant committee, information concerning any particular loan or guarantee, including all information necessary to show— (a) the extent of the total indebtedness by way of principal and accumulated interest; (b) the use made or to be made of the proceeds of the loan; (c) the provision made for servicing or repayment of the loan; and (d) the progress made in the repayment of the loan.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu borrowing by serikali ya kitaifa. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Parliament can, by legislation— (a) prescribe the terms on which the national government can borrow; and (b) impose reporting requirements. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Borrowing by national government, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Within seven days after either House of Parliament so requests by resolution, the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance must present to the relevant committee, information concerning any particular loan or guarantee, including all information necessary to show— (a) the extent of the total indebtedness by way of principal and accumulated interest; (b) the use made or to be made of the proceeds of the loan; (c) the provision made for servicing or repayment of the loan; and (d) the progress made in the repayment of the loan. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Borrowing by national government, that is how it should be handled.

articles.simplified: County government may borrow only if the national government guarantees it.

articles.title: Public debt
articles.simplified: (1) The public debt is a charge on the Consolidated Fund, but an Act of Parliament can provide for charging all or part of the public debt to other public funds. (2) For the purposes of this Article, "the public debt" means all financial obligations attendant to loans raised or guaranteed and securities issued or guaranteed by the national government.

articles.title: Commission on Revenue Allocation
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the Commission on Revenue Allocation. (2) The Commission must consist of the following persons appointed by the President— (a) a chairperson, who must be nominated by the President and approved by the National Assembly; (b) two persons nominated by the political parties represented in the National Assembly according to your proportion of members in the Assembly; (c) five persons nominated by the political parties represented in the Senate according to your proportion of members in the Senate; and (d) the Principal Secretary in the Ministry responsible for finance. (3) The persons nominated under clause (2) must not be members of Parliament.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu tume ya Ugavi wa Mapato. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Commission on Revenue Allocation, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Functions of the Commission on Revenue Allocation
articles.simplified: (1) The principal function of the Commission on Revenue Allocation is to make recommendations concerning the basis for the equitable sharing of revenue raised by the national government— (a) between the national and county governments; and (b) among the county governments. (2) The Commission must also make recommendations on other matters concerning the financing of, and financial management by, county governments, as required by this Constitution and national legislation. (3) In formulating recommendations, the Commission must seek— (a) to promote and give effect to the criteria set out in Article 203(1); (b) when appropriate, to define and enhance the revenue sources of the national and county governments; and (c) to encourage fiscal responsibility.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) The principal function of the Commission on Revenue Allocation is to make recommendations concerning the basis for the equitable sharing of revenue raised by the national government— (a) between the national and county governments; and (b) among the county governments. articles.title: Division of revenue
articles.simplified: (1) Once every five years, the Senate must, by resolution, determine the basis for allocating among the counties the share of national revenue that is annually allocated to the county level of government. (2) In determining the basis of revenue sharing under clause (1), the Senate must— (a) take the criteria in Article 203(1) into account; (b) request and consider recommendations from the Commission on Revenue Allocation; (c) consult the county governors, the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance and any organisation of county governments; and (d) invite the public, including professional bodies, to make submissions to it on the matter. (3) Within ten days after the Senate adopts a resolution under clause (1), the Speaker of the Senate must refer the resolution to the Speaker of the National Assembly.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu division of revenue. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Once every five years, the Senate must, by resolution, determine the basis for allocating among the counties the share of national revenue that is annually allocated to the county level of government. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Division of revenue, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) In determining the basis of revenue sharing under clause (1), the Senate must— (a) take the criteria in Article 203(1) into account; (b) request and consider recommendations from the Commission on Revenue Allocation; (c) consult the county governors, the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance and any organisation of county governments; and (d) invite the public, including professional bodies, to make submissions to it on the matter. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Division of revenue, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Annual Division and Allocation of Revenue Bills
articles.simplified: (1) At least two months before the end of each financial year, there must be introduced in Parliament— (a) a Division of Revenue Bill, which must divide revenue raised by the national government among the national and county levels of government in accordance with this Constitution; and (b) a County Allocation of Revenue Bill, which must divide among the counties the revenue allocated to the county level of government on the basis determined in accordance with the resolution in force under Article 217. (2) Each Bill required by clause (1) must be accompanied by a memorandum setting out— (a) an explanation of revenue allocation as proposed by the Bill; (b) an evaluation of the Bill in relation to the criteria set out in Article 203(1); and (c) a summary of any significant deviation from the Commission on Revenue Allocation’s recommendations, with an explanation for each such deviation.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Annual Division and Allocation of Revenue Bills, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Each Bill required by clause (1) must be accompanied by a memorandum setting out— (a) an explanation of revenue allocation as proposed by the Bill; (b) an evaluation of the Bill in relation to the criteria set out in Article 203(1); and (c) a summary of any significant deviation from the Commission on Revenue Allocation’s recommendations, with an explanation for each such deviation. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Annual Division and Allocation of Revenue Bills, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Transfer of equitable share
articles.simplified: A county’s share of revenue raised by the national government must be transferred to the county without undue delay and without deduction, except when the transfer has been stopped under Article 225. It also guides what officials must or must not do for you.

articles.title: Form, content and timing of budgets
articles.simplified: (1) Budgets of the national and county governments must contain— (a) estimates of revenue and expenditure, differentiating between recurrent and development expenditure; (b) proposals for financing any anticipated deficit for the period to which they apply; and (c) proposals regarding borrowing and other forms of public liability that will increase public debt during the following year. (2) National legislation must prescribe— (a) the structure of the development plans and budgets of counties; (b) when the plans and budgets of the counties must be tabled in the county assemblies; and (c) the form and manner of consultation between the national government and county governments in the process of preparing plans and budgets.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Form, content and timing of budgets, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) National legislation must prescribe— (a) the structure of the development plans and budgets of counties; (b) when the plans and budgets of the counties must be tabled in the county assemblies; and (c) the form and manner of consultation between the national government and county governments in the process of preparing plans and budgets. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Form, content and timing of budgets, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Budget estimates and annual Appropriation Bill
articles.simplified: (1) At least two months before the end of each financial year, the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance must submit to the National Assembly estimates of the revenue and expenditure of the national government for the next financial year to be tabled in the National Assembly. (2) The estimates referred to in clause (1) must— (a) include estimates for expenditure from the Equalisation Fund; and (b) be in the form, and according to the procedure, prescribed by an Act of Parliament. (3) The National Assembly must consider the estimates submitted under clause (1) together with the estimates submitted by the Parliamentary Service Commission and the Chief Registrar of the Judiciary under Articles 127 and 173 respectively.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) At least two months before the end of each financial year, the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance must submit to the National Assembly estimates of the revenue and expenditure of the national government for the next financial year to be tabled in the National Assembly. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Budget estimates and annual Appropriation Bill, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The estimates referred to in clause (1) must— (a) include estimates for expenditure from the Equalisation Fund; and (b) be in the form, and according to the procedure, prescribed by an Act of Parliament. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Budget estimates and annual Appropriation Bill, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Expenditure before annual budget is passed
articles.simplified: (1) If the Appropriation Act for a financial year has not been assented to, or is not likely to be assented to, by the beginning of that financial year, the National Assembly can authorise the withdrawal of money from the Consolidated Fund. (2) Money withdrawn under clause (1) must— (a) be for the purpose of meeting expenditure necessary to carry on the services of the national government during that year until such time as the Appropriation Act is assented to; (b) not exceed in total one-half of the amount included in the estimates of expenditure for that year that have been tabled in the National Assembly; and (c) be included, under separate votes for the several services in respect of which they were withdrawn, in the Appropriation Act.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) If the Appropriation Act for a financial year has not been assented to, or is not likely to be assented to, by the beginning of that financial year, the National Assembly can authorise the withdrawal of money from the Consolidated Fund. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Expenditure before annual budget is passed, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) Money withdrawn under clause (1) must— (a) be for the purpose of meeting expenditure necessary to carry on the services of the national government during that year until such time as the Appropriation Act is assented to; (b) not exceed in total one-half of the amount included in the estimates of expenditure for that year that have been tabled in the National Assembly; and (c) be included, under separate votes for the several services in respect of which they were withdrawn, in the Appropriation Act. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Expenditure before annual budget is passed, that is how it should be handled.

articles.title: Supplementary appropriation
articles.simplified: (1) Subject to clauses (2) to (4), the national government can spend money that has not been appropriated if— (a) the amount appropriated for any purpose under the Appropriation Act is insufficient or a need has arisen for expenditure for a purpose for which no amount has been appropriated by that Act; or (b) money has been withdrawn from the Contingencies Fund. (2) The approval of Parliament for any spending under this Article must be sought within two months after the first withdrawal of the money, subject to clause (3). (3) If Parliament is not sitting during the time contemplated in clause (2), or is sitting but adjourns before the approval has been sought, the approval must be sought within two weeks after it next sits.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu supplementary appropriation. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) Subject to clauses (2) to (4), the national government can spend money that has not been appropriated if— (a) the amount appropriated for any purpose under the Appropriation Act is insufficient or a need has arisen for expenditure for a purpose for which no amount has been appropriated by that Act; or (b) money has been withdrawn from the Contingencies Fund. That would break this protection, and they can challenge it. Under this part on Supplementary appropriation, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The approval of Parliament for any spending under this Article must be sought within two months after the first withdrawal of the money, subject to clause (3). That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Supplementary appropriation, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu supplementary appropriation.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu supplementary appropriation.

articles.title: County appropriation Bills
articles.simplified: On the basis of the Division of Revenue Bill passed by Parliament under Article 218, each county government must prepare and adopt its own annual budget and appropriation Bill in the form, and according to the procedure, prescribed in an Act of Parliament. It also guides what officials must or must not do for you.

articles.title: Financial control
articles.simplified: (1) An Act of Parliament must provide for the establishment, functions and responsibilities of the national Treasury. (2) Parliament must enact legislation to ensure both expenditure control and transparency in all governments and establish mechanisms to ensure your implementation. (3) Legislation under clause (2) can authorise the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance to stop the transfer of funds to a State organ or any other public entity— (a) only for a serious material breach or persistent material breaches of the measures established under that legislation; and (b) subject to the requirements of clauses (4) to (7).
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu financial control. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.

That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Financial control, that is how it should be handled.
swExamples: Ibara hii inaonyesha sheria ya msingi kuhusu financial control.
Mfano wa maisha ya kila siku: sheria hii inatumika unapoona tatizo linalohusu financial control.

articles.title: Accounts and audit of public entities
articles.simplified: (1) An Act of Parliament must provide for— (a) the keeping of financial records and the auditing of accounts of all governments and other public entities, and prescribe other measures for securing efficient and transparent fiscal management; and (b) the designation of an accounting officer in every public entity at the national and county level of government. (2) The accounting officer of a national public entity is accountable to the National Assembly for its financial management, and the accounting officer of a county public entity is accountable to the county assembly for its financial management. (3) Subject to clause (4), the accounts of all governments and State organs must be audited by the Auditor-General.
articles.swSimplified: Ibara hii inaeleza kwa ufupi kuhusu ibara hii. Soma maandishi rasmi hapa chini kwa maelezo kamili.
examples: Kamau is in a real situation: (1) An Act of Parliament must provide for— (a) the keeping of financial records and the auditing of accounts of all governments and other public entities, and prescribe other measures for securing efficient and transparent fiscal management; and (b) the designation of an accounting officer in every public entity at the national and county level of government. That fits this protection and should happen without being stopped. Under this part on Accounts and audit of public entities, that is how it should be handled.

Achieng is in a real situation: (2) The accounting officer of a national public entity is accountable to the National Assembly for its financial management, and the accounting officer of a county public entity is accountable to the county assembly for its financial management. articles.title: Procurement of public goods and services
articles.simplified: (1) When a State organ or any other public entity contracts for goods or services, it must do so in accordance with a system that is fair, equitable, transparent, competitive and cost-effective. (2) An Act of Parliament must prescribe a framework within which policies relating to procurement and asset disposal must be implemented and can provide for all or any of the following— (a) categories of preference in the allocation of contracts; (b) the protection or advancement of persons, categories of persons or groups previously disadvantaged by unfair competition or discrimination; (c) sanctions against contractors that have not performed according to professionally regulated procedures, contractual agreements or legislation; and (d) sanctions against persons who have defaulted on your tax obligations, or have been guilty of corrupt practices or serious violations of fair employment laws and practices.

articles.title: Controller of Budget
articles.simplified: (1) There must be a Controller of Budget who must be nominated by the President and, with the approval of the National Assembly, appointed by the President. (2) To be qualified to be the Controller, You must have extensive knowledge of public finance or at least ten years' experience in auditing public finance management. (3) The Controller must, subject to Article 251, hold office for a term of eight years and must not be eligible for re-appointment.

articles.title: Salaries and Remuneration Commission
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the Salaries and Remuneration Commission. (2) The Salaries and Remuneration Commission consists of the following persons appointed by the President— (a) a chairperson; (b) one person each nominated by the following bodies from among persons who are not members or employees of those bodies— (i) the Parliamentary Service Commission; (ii) the Public Service Commission; (iii) the Judicial Service Commission; (iv) the Teachers Service Commission; (v) the National Police Service Commission; (vi) the Defence Council; and (vii) the Senate, on behalf of the county governments; (c) one person each nominated by— (i) an umbrella body representing trade unions; (ii) an umbrella body representing employers; and (iii) a joint forum of professional bodies as provided by legislation; (d) one person each nominated by— (i) the Cabinet Secretary responsible for finance; and (ii) the Attorney-General; and (e) one person who has experience in the management of human resources in the public service, nominated by the Cabinet Secretary responsible for public service. (3) The Commissioners under clause (2)(d) and (e) must have no vote.

articles.title: Central Bank of Kenya
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the Central Bank of Kenya. (2) The Central Bank of Kenya must be responsible for formulating monetary policy, promoting price stability, issuing currency and performing other functions conferred on it by an Act of Parliament. (3) The Central Bank of Kenya must not be under the direction or control of You or authority in the exercise of its powers or in the performance of its functions.

articles.title: The Public Service Commission
articles.simplified: (1) There is established the Public Service Commission. (2) The Public Service Commission consists of a chairperson, a vice chairperson and seven other members appointed by the President with the approval of the National Assembly. (3) Subject to clause (4), You is not eligible for appointment as a member of the Commission if the person–— (a) has, at any time within the preceding five years, held office, or stood for election as— (i) a member of Parliament or of a county assembly; or (ii) a member of the governing body of a political party; or (b) holds any State office; (c) is, or has at any time been, a candidate for election as a member of Parliament or of a county assembly; or (d) is, or has at any time been, the holder of an office in any political organisation that sponsors or otherwise supports, or has at any time sponsored or otherwise supported, a candidate for election as a member of Parliament or of a county assembly.

articles.title: Functions and powers of the Public Service Commission
articles.simplified: (1) The functions and powers of the Commission are as set out in this Article. (2) The Commission must— (a) subject to this Constitution and legislation— (i) establish and abolish offices in the public service; and (ii) appoint persons to hold or act in those offices, and to confirm appointments; (b) exercise disciplinary control over and remove persons holding or acting in those offices; (c) promote the values and principles referred to in Articles 10 and 232 throughout the public service; (d) investigate, monitor and evaluate the organisation, administration and personnel practices of the public service; (e) ensure that the public service is efficient and effective; (f) develop human resources in the public service; (g) review and make recommendations to the national government in respect of conditions of service, code of conduct and qualifications of officers in the public service; (h) evaluate and report to the President and Parliament on the extent to which the values and principles referred to in Articles 10 and 232 are complied with in the public service; (i) hear and determine appeals in respect of county governments’ public service; and (j) perform any other functions and exercise any other powers conferred by national legislation. (3) Clauses (1) and (2) must not apply to any of the following offices in the public service— (a) State offices; (b) an office of high commissioner, ambassador or other diplomatic or consular representative of the Republic; (c) an office or position subject to— (i) the Parliamentary Service Commission; (ii) the Judicial Service Commission; (iii) the Teachers Service Commission; (iv) the National Police Service Commission; or (d) an office in the service of a county government, except as contemplated in clause (2)(i).

articles.title: Protection of public officers
articles.simplified: A public officer must not be— (a) victimised or discriminated against for having performed the functions of office in accordance with this Constitution or any other law; or (b) dismissed, removed from office, demoted in rank or otherwise subjected to disciplinary action without due process of law. It also guides what officials must or must not do for you.
